text.NRSV: All wisdom is from the Lord, and with him it remains forever.
text.DRB: All wisdom is of our Lord God, & hath been always with him, & is before all time.
text.KJV: 1:1 This Iesus was the sonne of Sirach, and grand-childe to Iesus of the same name with him; This man therefore liued in the latter times, after the people had bene led away captiue, and called home againe, and almost after all the Prophets. Now his grandfather Iesus (as he himselfe witnesseth) was a man of great diligence and wisedome among the Hebrewes, who did not onely gather the graue and short Sentences of wise men, that had bene before him, but himselfe also vttered some of his owne, full of much vnderstanding and wisedome. When as therefore the first Iesus died, leauing this booke almost perfected, Si rach his sonne receiuing it after him, left it to his owne sonne Iesus, who hauing gotten it into his hands, compiled it all orderly into one Volume, and called it Wisdome, Intituling it, both by his owne name, his fathers name, and his grandfathers, alluring the hearer by the very name of Wisedome, to haue a greater loue to the studie of this Booke. It conteineth therefore wise Sayings, darke Sentences, and Parables, and certaine particular ancient godly stories of men that pleased God. Also his Prayer and Song. Moreouer, what benefits God had vouchsafed his people, and what plagues he had heaped vpon their enemies. This Iesus did imitate Solomon, and was no lesse famous for Wisedome, and learning, both being indeed a man of great learning, and so reputed also. Whereas many and great things haue bene deliuered vnto vs by the Law and the Prophets, and by others that haue followed their steps, for the which things Israel ought to be commended for learning and Wisedome, and whereof not onely the Readers must needs become skilful themselues, but also they that desire to learne, be able to profit them which are without, both by speaking and writing : My grandfather Iesus, when he had much giuen himselfe to the reading of the Law, and the Prophets, and other Bookes of our fathers, and had gotten therein good iudgement, was drawen on also himselfe, to write something pertayning to learning and Wisedome, to the intent that those which are desirous to learne, and are addicted to these things, might profit much more in liuing according to the Law. Wherefore, let me intreat you to reade it with fauour and attention, and to pardon Vs, wherein wee may secme to come short of some words which we haue laboured to interprete. For the same things vttered in Hebrew, and translated into an other tongue, haue not the same force in them : and not onely these things, but the Law it selfe, and the Prophets, and the rest of the Bookes, haue no small difference, when they are spoken in their owne language. For in the eight and thirtieth yeere comming into Egypt, when Euergetes was King, and continuing there some time, I found a Booke of no small learning, therefore I thought it most necessary for mee, to bestow some diligence and trauaile to interprete it: Vsing great watchfulnesse, and skill in that space, to bring the Booke to an end, and set it foorth for them also, which in a strange countrey are willing to learne, being prepared before in maners to line after the Law. All wisedome commeth from the Lord, and is with him for euer.
text.NABRE: Foreword Inasmuch as many and great truths have been given to us through the Law, the prophets, and the authors who followed them, for which the instruction and wisdom of Israel merit praise, it is the duty of those who read the scriptures not only to become knowledgeable themselves but also to use their love of learning in speech and in writing to help others less familiar. So my grandfather Jesus, who had long devoted himself to the study of the law, the prophets, and the rest of the books of our ancestors, and had acquired great familiarity with them, was moved to write something himself regarding instruction and wisdom. He did this so that those who love learning might, by accepting what he had written, make even greater progress in living according to the Law. You are invited therefore to read it with good will and attention, with indulgence for any failure on our part, despite earnest efforts, in the interpretation of particular passages. For words spoken originally in Hebrew do not have the same effect when they are translated into another language. That is true not only of this book but of the Law itself, the prophecies, and the rest of the books, which differ no little when they are read in the original. I arrived in Egypt in the thirty-eighth year of the reign of King Euergetes, and while there, I had access to no little learning. I therefore considered it my duty to devote some diligence and industry to the translation of this book. During this time I applied my skill for many sleepless hours to complete the book and publish it for those living abroad who wish to acquire learning and are disposed to live their lives according to the Law. The Wisdom of Ben Sira Chapter 1 God’s Gift of Wisdom All wisdom is from the Lord and remains with him forever.

text.NRSV: The sand of the sea, the drops of rain, and the days of eternity—who can count them?
text.DRB: The sand of the sea, & the drops of rain, & the days of the world, who hath numbered? The height of heaven, and breadth of the earth, & profundity of the depth, who hath measured?
text.KJV: 1:2 Who can number the sand of the sea, and the drops of raine, and the dayes of eternity?
text.NABRE: The sands of the sea, the drops of rain, the days of eternity—who can count them?

text.NRSV: The height of heaven, the breadth of the earth, the abyss, and wisdom—who can search them out?
text.DRB: The wisdom of God that goeth before all things, who hath searched out?
text.KJV: 1:3 Who can finde out the height of heauen, and the breadth of the earth, and the deepe, and wisedome?
text.NABRE: Heaven’s height, earth’s extent, the abyss and wisdom—who can explore them?

text.NRSV: Wisdom was created before all other things, and prudent understanding from eternity.
text.DRB: Wisdom was created before all things, & the understanding of prudence from everlasting.
text.KJV: 1:4 Wisedome hath beene created before all things, and the vnderstanding of prudence from euerlasting.
text.NABRE: Before all other things wisdom was created; and prudent understanding, from eternity.

text.DRB: Afountain of wisdom the word of God on high, and the entrance thereof everlasting commandments.
text.KJV: 1:5 The word of God most high, is the fountaine of wisdome, & her wayes are euerlasting commandements.

text.NRSV: The root of wisdom—to whom has it been revealed? Her subtleties—who knows them?
text.DRB: The root of wisdom to whom hath it been revealed, & the subtilties thereof who hath known?
text.KJV: 1:6 To whom hath the root of wisdome beene reuealed? or who hath knowen her wise counsels?

text.DRB: The discipline of wisdom to whom hath it been revealed and made manifest, and the multiplication of her entrance who hath understood.

text.NRSV: There is but one who is wise, greatly to be feared, seated upon his throne—the Lord.
text.DRB: There is one most high Creator omnipotent, and mighty King, and to be feared exceedingly, sitting upon his throne & the God of dominion.
text.KJV: 1:8 There is one wise and greatly to bee feared; the Lord sitting vpon his Throne.

text.NRSV: It is he who created her; he saw her and took her measure; he poured her out upon all his works,
text.DRB: He created her in the Holy Ghost, and hath seen, and numbered, and measured her.
text.KJV: 1:9 He created her, and saw her, and numbred her, and powred her out vpon all his workes.

text.NRSV: upon all the living according to his gift; he lavished her upon those who love him.
text.DRB: And he hath poured her out upon all his works, and upon all flesh according to his gift, and hath given her to them that fear him.
text.KJV: 1:10 Shee with all flesh according to his gift, and hee hath giuen her to them that loue him.

text.NRSV: The fear of the Lord is glory and exultation, and gladness and a crown of rejoicing.
text.DRB: The fear of our Lord is glory, and gloriation, and joy, and a crown of exultation.
text.KJV: 1:11 The feare of the Lord is honour, and glory, and gladnesse, and a crowne of reioycing.

text.NRSV: The fear of the Lord delights the heart, and gives gladness and joy and long life.
text.DRB: The fear of our Lord shall delight the heart, and shall give joy, and gladness, and length of days.
text.KJV: 1:12 The feare of the Lord maketh a merrie heart, and giueth ioy and gladnesse, and a long life.

text.DRB: With him that feareth our Lord it shall be well in the later end, and in the day of his death he shall be blessed.
text.KJV: 1:13 Who so feareth the Lord, it shall goe well with him at the last, & he shall finde fauour in the day of his death.

text.NRSV: To fear the Lord is the beginning of wisdom; she is created with the faithful in the womb.
text.DRB: The love of God is honourable wisdom.
text.KJV: 1:14 To feare the Lord, is the beginning of wisedome: and it was created with the faithfull in the wombe.

text.DRB: But they to whom she shall appear in vision, they love her in the vision, and in the agnizing of her great works.
text.KJV: 1:15 Shee hath built an euerlasting foundation with men, and she shal continue with their seede.

text.DRB: The fear of our Lord is the beginning of wisdom, and was created with the faithful in the womb, and goeth with the elect women, and is known with the just & faithful.
text.KJV: 1:16 To feare the Lord, is fulnesse of wisedome, and filleth men with her fruits.

text.DRB: The fear of our Lord is religiosity of knowledge.
text.KJV: 1:17 Shee filleth all their house with things desireable, and the garners with her increase.

text.NRSV: The fear of the Lord is the crown of wisdom, making peace and perfect health to flourish.
text.DRB: Religiosity shall keep and justify the heart, shall give joy and gladness.
text.KJV: 1:18 The feare of the Lord is a crowne of wisedome, making peace and perfect health to flourish, both which are the gifts of God: and it enlargeth their reioycing that loue him.

text.DRB: With him that feareth our Lord it shall be well, and in the days of his consummation he shall be blessed.

text.KJV: 1:20 The root of wisedome is to feare the Lord, and the branches thereof are long life.
text.NRSV: To fear the Lord is the root of wisdom, and her branches are long life.
text.DRB: The fullness of wisdom is to fear God, and fullness is of the fruits thereof.
All her house she shall fill with her generations, and the store-houses with her treasures.

text.KJV: 1:22 A furious man cannot be iustified, for the sway of his fury shalbe his destruction.
text.NRSV: Unjust anger cannot be justified, for anger tips the scale to one’s ruin.
text.DRB: Acrown of wisdom, the fear of our Lord, replenishing peace, and the fruit of salvation:
and hath seen, and numbered her: but both are the gifts of God.
Wisdom shall distribute knowledge, and understanding of prudence: and exalteth the glory of them that hold it.
The root of wisdom is to fear our Lord: for the boughs thereof are of long time.

text.KJV: 1:26 If thou desire wisedome, keepe the commaundements, and the Lord shall giue her vnto thee.
text.NRSV: If you desire wisdom, keep the commandments, and the Lord will lavish her upon you.
text.DRB: in the treasures of wisdom is understanding, & religiosity of knowledge: but to sinners wisdom is abomination.

text.KJV: 1:27 For the feare of the Lord is wisdome, and instruction: and faith and meekenesse are his delight.
text.NRSV: For the fear of the Lord is wisdom and discipline; faithfulness and humility are his delight.
text.DRB: The fear of our Lord expelleth sin:

text.KJV: 1:28 Distrust not the feare of the Lord when thou art poore: and come not vnto him with a double heart.
text.NRSV: Do not disobey the fear of the Lord; do not approach him with a divided mind.
text.DRB: for he that is without fear, cannot be justified: for the anger of his animosity, is his subversion.
Until a time the patient shall sustain, and after shall be rewarded of joyfulness.

text.KJV: 1:30 Exalt not thy selfe, lest thou fall, and bring dishonor vpon thy soule, and so God discouer thy secrets, and cast thee downe in the midst of the congregation, because thou camest not in trueth, to the feare of the Lord: but thy heart is full of deceit.
text.NRSV: Do not exalt yourself, or you may fall and bring dishonor upon yourself.
text.DRB: Agood understanding will hide his words until a time, and the lips of many shall shew forth his understanding.
In the treasures of wisdom is signification of discipline:
But the worship of God is abomination to a sinner.
Son, coveting wisdom, keep justice, & God will give her to thee.
For the fear of our Lord is wisdom and discipline: and that which well pleaseth him,
is faith, and meekness, and he will fill his treasures.
Be not incredulous to the fear of our Lord: and come not to him with a double heart.
Be not an hypocrite in the sight of men, and be not scandalized in thy lips.
Attend to them, lest perhaps thou fall, and bring dishonour to thy soul,
and God reveal thy secrets, and in the midst of the synagogue cast thee down:
because thou camest to our Lord wickedly, & thy heart is full of guile and deceit.

text.KJV: 2:1 My sonne, if thou come to serue the Lorde, prepare thy soule for temptation.
text.NABRE: Chapter 2 Trust in God My child, when you come to serve the Lord, prepare yourself for trials.
text.NRSV: My child, when you come to serve the Lord, prepare yourself for testing.
text.DRB: Son, coming to the service of God, stand in justice, and in fear, & prepare thy soul to tentation.

text.KJV: 2:2 Set thy heart aright, and constantly endure, and make not haste in time of trouble.
text.NABRE: Be sincere of heart and steadfast, and do not be impetuous in time of adversity.
text.NRSV: Set your heart right and be steadfast, and do not be impetuous in time of calamity.
text.DRB: Repress thy heart, & sustain: incline thine ear, & receive the words of understanding, and make no haste in the time of obduction.

text.KJV: 2:3 Cleaue vnto him, and depart not away, that thou mayest be increased at thy last end.
text.NABRE: Cling to him, do not leave him, that you may prosper in your last days.
text.NRSV: Cling to him and do not depart, so that your last days may be prosperous.
text.DRB: Sustain the sustentations of God: be joined to God, and sustain, that thy life may increase in the later end.

text.KJV: 2:4 Whatsoeuer is brought vpon thee, take cheerefully, and bee patient when thou art changed to a lowe estate.
text.NABRE: Accept whatever happens to you; in periods of humiliation be patient.
text.NRSV: Accept whatever befalls you, and in times of humiliation be patient.
text.DRB: All that shall be applied to thee, receive: and in sorrow sustain, and in thy humiliation have patience:

text.KJV: 2:5 For gold is tried in the fire, and acceptable men in the furnace of aduersitie.
text.NABRE: For in fire gold is tested, and the chosen, in the crucible of humiliation.
text.NRSV: For gold is tested in the fire, and those found acceptable, in the furnace of humiliation.
text.DRB: for gold and silver are tried in the fire, but acceptable men in the furnace of humiliation.

text.KJV: 2:6 Beleeue in him, and he will helpe thee, order thy way aright, and trust in him.
text.NABRE: Trust in God, and he will help you; make your ways straight and hope in him.
text.NRSV: Trust in him, and he will help you; make your ways straight, and hope in him.
text.DRB: Believe God, and he will recover thee: and direct thy way, and hope in him. Keep his fear, and grow old therein.

text.KJV: 2:7 Ye that feare the Lord, waite for his mercie, and goe not aside, lest ye fall.
text.NABRE: You that fear the Lord, wait for his mercy, do not stray lest you fall.
text.NRSV: You who fear the Lord, wait for his mercy; do not stray, or you may fall.
text.DRB: Ye that fear our Lord, expect his mercy: & decline not from him, lest ye fall.

text.KJV: 2:8 Yee that feare the Lord, beleeue him, and your reward shall not faile.
text.NABRE: You that fear the Lord, trust in him, and your reward will not be lost.
text.NRSV: You who fear the Lord, trust in him, and your reward will not be lost.
text.DRB: Ye that fear our Lord, believe him, & your reward shall not be void.

text.KJV: 2:9 Ye that feare the Lord, hope for good, and for euerlasting ioy and mercy.
text.NABRE: You that fear the Lord, hope for good things, for lasting joy and mercy.
text.NRSV: You who fear the Lord, hope for good things, for lasting joy and mercy.
text.DRB: Ye that fear our Lord hope in him: and mercy shall come to you for your delectation.

text.KJV: 2:10 Looke at the generations of old, and see, did euer any trust in the Lord, and was confounded? or did any abide in his feare, & was forsaken? or whom did hee euer despise, that called vpon him?
text.NABRE: Consider the generations long past and see: has anyone trusted in the Lord and been disappointed? Has anyone persevered in his fear and been forsaken? has anyone called upon him and been ignored?
text.NRSV: Consider the ancient generations and see: who ever trusted in the Lord and was put to shame? Or who ever persevered in the fear of the Lord and was forsaken? Or who ever called upon him and was overlooked?
text.DRB: Ye that fear our Lord, love him, & your hearts shall be illuminated.

text.KJV: 2:11 For the Lord is full of compassion, and mercie, longsuffering, and very pitifull, and forgiueth sinnes, and saueth in time of affliction.
text.NABRE: For the Lord is compassionate and merciful; forgives sins and saves in time of trouble.
text.NRSV: For the Lord is compassionate and merciful; he forgives sins and saves in time of distress.
text.DRB: Children behold the nations of men: and know ye that none hath hoped in our Lord, and hath been confounded.

text.KJV: 2:12 Woe be to fearefull hearts, and faint hands, and the sinner that goeth two wayes.
text.NABRE: Woe to timid hearts and drooping hands, to the sinner who walks a double path!
text.NRSV: Woe to timid hearts and to slack hands, and to the sinner who walks a double path!
text.DRB: For who hath continued in his commandment, and hath been forsaken? Or who hath invocated him, and he despised him?

text.KJV: 2:13 Woe vnto him that is faint hearted, for he beleeueth not, therefore shall he not be defended.
text.NABRE: Woe to the faint of heart! For they do not trust, and therefore have no shelter!
text.NRSV: Woe to the fainthearted who have no trust! Therefore they will have no shelter.
text.DRB: Because God is pitiful and merciful, and will forgive sins in the day of tribulation: and he is protector to all that seek him in truth.

text.KJV: 2:14 Woe vnto you that haue lost patience: and what will ye doe when the Lord shall visite you?
text.NABRE: Woe to you that have lost hope! what will you do at the Lord’s visitation?
text.NRSV: Woe to you who have lost your nerve! What will you do when the Lord’s reckoning comes?
text.DRB: Woe to them of a double heart, & to wicked lips, and to the hands that do evil, and to the sinner that goeth on the earth two ways.

text.KJV: 2:15 They that feare the Lord, will not disobey his word, and they that loue him, will keepe his wayes.
text.NABRE: Those who fear the Lord do not disobey his words; those who love him keep his ways.
text.NRSV: Those who fear the Lord do not disobey his words, and those who love him keep his ways.
text.DRB: Woe to them that be dissolute of heart, which believe not God: and therefore they shall not be protected of him.
Woe be to them, that have lost patience, and that have forsaken the right ways, and have declined into perverse ways.
text.NABRE: Those who fear the Lord seek to please him; those who love him are filled with his law.

text.KJV: 2:17 They that feare the Lord, will prepare their hearts, and humble their soules in his sight:
text.NABRE: Those who fear the Lord prepare their hearts and humble themselves before him.
text.NRSV: Those who fear the Lord prepare their hearts, and humble themselves before him.
text.DRB: And what will they do, when our Lord shall begin to look on them?

text.KJV: 2:18 Saying, We will fal into the hands of the Lord, and not into the hands of men: for as his maiestie is, so is his mercie.
text.NABRE: Let us fall into the hands of the Lord and not into the hands of mortals, For equal to his majesty is his mercy; and equal to his name are his works.
text.NRSV: Let us fall into the hands of the Lord, but not into the hands of mortals; for equal to his majesty is his mercy, and equal to his name are his works.
text.DRB: They that fear our Lord, will not be incredulous to his word: and they that love him, will keep his way.
They that fear our Lord, will seek after the things that are well pleasing to him: and they that love him, shall be filled with his law.
They that fear our Lord, will prepare their hearts, and in his sight will sanctify their souls.
They that fear our Lord, keep his commandments, and will have patience even until his visitation,
saying: If we do not penance, we shall fall into the hands of our Lord, and not into the hands of men.
For according to his greatness, so also his mercy is with him.

text.KJV: 3:1 Heare mee your father, O children, and doe thereafter, that ye may be safe.
text.NABRE: Chapter 3 Responsibilities to Parents Children, listen to me, your father; act accordingly, that you may be safe.
text.NRSV: Listen to me your father, O children; act accordingly, that you may be kept in safety.
text.DRB: The children of wisdom are the Church of the just: and their nation, obedience and love.

text.KJV: 3:2 For the Lord hath giuen the father honour ouer the children, and hath confirmed the authoritie of the mother ouer the sonnes.
text.NABRE: For the Lord sets a father in honor over his children and confirms a mother’s authority over her sons.
text.NRSV: For the Lord honors a father above his children, and he confirms a mother’s right over her sons.
text.DRB: Hear your father's judgement o children, and so do that you may be saved.

text.KJV: 3:3 Who so honoureth his father, maketh an atonement for his sinnes.
text.NABRE: Those who honor their father atone for sins;
text.NRSV: Those who honor their father atone for sins,
text.DRB: For God hath honoured the father in the children: and inquiring of the mother's judgement, hath confirmed it upon the children.

text.KJV: 3:4 And he that honoureth his mother, is as one that layeth vp treasure.
text.NABRE: they store up riches who respect their mother.
text.NRSV: and those who respect their mother are like those who lay up treasure.
text.DRB: He that loveth God, praying shall obtain for sins, and shall refrain himself from them, and shall be heard in the prayer of days.
And as he that gathereth treasure, so he also that honoureth his mother.
text.NABRE: Those who honor their father will have joy in their own children, and when they pray they are heard.

text.DRB: He that honoureth his father, shall have joy in children, and in the day of his prayer he shall be heard.
text.NABRE: Those who respect their father will live a long life; those who obey the Lord honor their mother.

text.DRB: He that honoureth his father, shall live the longer life: & he that obeyeth the father, shall refresh the mother.
text.NABRE: Those who fear the Lord honor their father, and serve their parents as masters.

text.KJV: 3:8 Honour thy father and mother, both in word and deed, that a blessing may come vpon thee from them.
text.NABRE: In word and deed honor your father, that all blessings may come to you.
text.NRSV: Honor your father by word and deed, that his blessing may come upon you.
text.DRB: He that feareth our Lord honoureth his parents, and as his lords he will serve them that begat him.

text.KJV: 3:9 For the blessing of the father establisheth the houses of children, but the curse of the mother rooteth out foundations.
text.NABRE: A father’s blessing gives a person firm roots, but a mother’s curse uproots the growing plant.
text.NRSV: For a father’s blessing strengthens the houses of the children, but a mother’s curse uproots their foundations.
text.DRB: In work and word, & all patience honour thy father,
that blessing may come upon thee from him, & his blessing may remain in the later end.
text.NABRE: Do not glory in your father’s disgrace, for that is no glory to you!

text.DRB: The father's blessing establisheth the houses of the children: but the mother's curse rooteth up the foundation.
text.NABRE: A father’s glory is glory also for oneself; they multiply sin who demean their mother.

text.KJV: 3:12 My sonne, helpe thy father in his age, and grieue him not as long as hee liueth.
text.NABRE: My son, be steadfast in honoring your father; do not grieve him as long as he lives.
text.NRSV: My child, help your father in his old age, and do not grieve him as long as he lives;
text.DRB: Glory not in the contumely of thy father: for his confusion is no glory to thee.

text.KJV: 3:13 And if his vnderstanding faile, haue patience with him, and despise him not, when thou art in thy ful strength.
text.NABRE: Even if his mind fails, be considerate of him; do not revile him because you are in your prime.
text.NRSV: even if his mind fails, be patient with him; because you have all your faculties do not despise him.
text.DRB: For the glory of a man is by the honour of his father, and the father without honour is the dishonour of the son.
Son receive the old age of thy father, and make him not sorrowful in his life:
text.NABRE: Kindness to a father will not be forgotten; it will serve as a sin offering—it will take lasting root.

text.DRB: and if he fail in understanding, pardon him, and despise him not in thy strength: for the alms to the father shall not be in oblivion.
text.NABRE: In time of trouble it will be recalled to your advantage, like warmth upon frost it will melt away your sins.

text.DRB: For good shall be restored thee for the sin of thy mother,
text.NABRE: Those who neglect their father are like blasphemers; those who provoke their mother are accursed by their Creator.

text.KJV: 3:17 My sonne, goe on with thy businesse in meekenesse, so shalt thou be beloued of him that is approued.
text.NABRE: Humility My son, conduct your affairs with humility, and you will be loved more than a giver of gifts.
text.NRSV: My child, perform your tasks with humility; then you will be loved by those whom God accepts.
text.DRB: and in justice it shall be builded to thee, and in the day of tribulation there shall be remembrance of thee: and as ice in the clear weather shall thy sins melt away.

text.KJV: 3:18 The greater thou art, the more humble thy selfe, and thou shalt find fauour before the Lord.
text.NABRE: Humble yourself the more, the greater you are, and you will find mercy in the sight of God.
text.NRSV: The greater you are, the more you must humble yourself; so you will find favor in the sight of the Lord.
text.DRB: Of what an evil fame is he, that forsaketh his father: and he is cursed of God, that doth exasperate his mother.
Son do thy works in meekness, and thou shalt be beloved above the glory of men.
The greater thou art, humble thyself in all things, and thou shalt find grace before God:

text.KJV: 3:21 Seeke not out the things that are too hard for thee, neither search the things that are aboue thy strength.
text.NRSV: Neither seek what is too difficult for you, nor investigate what is beyond your power.
text.DRB: because the might of God only is great, and he is honoured of the humble.

text.KJV: 3:22 But what is commaunded thee, thinke thereupon with reuerence, for it is not needfull for thee, to see with thine eyes, the things that are in secret.
text.NRSV: Reflect upon what has been assigned to you, for you do not need what is hidden.
text.DRB: Seek not things higher than thyself, and search not things stronger than thy hability: but the things that God hath commanded thee, think on them always, and in many of his works be not curious.
For it is not necessary for thee, to see with thine eyes those things that are hid.
In superfluous things search not many ways, and in many of his works thou shalt not be curious.
For very many things are shewed to thee above the understanding of men.

text.KJV: 3:26 A stubborne heart shall fare euill at the last, and he that loueth danger shall perish therein.
text.NRSV: A stubborn mind will fare badly at the end, and whoever loves danger will perish in it.
text.DRB: Many also have their suspicion supplanted, and have held their senses in vanity.
Ahard heart shall fare ill in the later end, and he that loveth danger, shall perish in it.
Aheart that goeth two ways, shall not have success, and the perverse of heart shall be scandalized in them.
Awicked heart shall be laden with sorrows, and the sinner will add to commit sin.

text.KJV: 3:30 Water will quench a flaming fire, and almes maketh an attonement for sinnes.
text.NRSV: As water extinguishes a blazing fire, so almsgiving atones for sin.
text.DRB: To the synagogue of the proud there shall be no health: for the shrub of sin shall be rooted up in them, and it shall not be perceived.
The heart of the wise is understood in wisdom, and a good ear will hear wisdom with all desire.
Awise heart, and that which hath understanding will abstain it-self from sins, and in the works of justice shall have success.
Water quencheth burning fire, and alms resisteth sins:
and God is the beholder of him that rendereth grace: he remembereth him afterward, and in the time of his fall he shall find a sure stay.

text.KJV: 4:1 My sonne, defraude not the poore of his liuing, and make not the needy eies to waite long.
text.NABRE: Chapter 4 My child, do not mock the life of the poor; do not keep needy eyes waiting.
text.NRSV: My child, do not cheat the poor of their living, and do not keep needy eyes waiting.
text.DRB: Son defraud not the alms of the poor, and turn not away thine eyes from the poor.

text.KJV: 4:2 Make not an hungry soule sorrowfull, neither prouoke a man in his distresse.
text.NABRE: Do not grieve the hungry, nor anger the needy.
text.NRSV: Do not grieve the hungry, or anger one in need.
text.DRB: Despise not the hungry soul: and exasperate not the poor in his poverty.
Afflict not the heart of the needy, & defer not the gift to him that is in distress.
text.NABRE: Do not aggravate a heart already angry, nor delay giving to the needy.

text.DRB: Reject not the petition of him that is afflicted: and turn not away thy face from the needy.
text.NABRE: A beggar’s request do not reject; do not turn your face away from the poor.

text.DRB: From the poor turn not away thine eyes for anger: and leave not to them that ask of thee, to curse thee behind thy back.
text.NABRE: From the needy do not turn your eyes; do not give them reason to curse you.

text.KJV: 4:6 For if he curse thee in the bitternesse of his soule, his prayer shall be heard of him that made him.
text.NABRE: If in their pain they cry out bitterly, their Rock will hear the sound of their cry.
text.NRSV: For if in bitterness of soul some should curse you, their Creator will hear their prayer.
text.DRB: For the prayer of him that curseth thee in the bitterness of his soul shall be heard: and he that made him, will hear him.

text.KJV: 4:7 Get thy selfe the loue of the congregation, and bow thy head to a great man.
text.NABRE: Social Conduct Endear yourself to the assembly; before the city’s ruler bow your head.
text.NRSV: Endear yourself to the congregation; bow your head low to the great.
text.DRB: Make thyself affable to the congregation of the poor, and to the ancient humble thy soul, and to a great man bow thy head.

text.KJV: 4:8 Let it not grieue thee to bowe downe thine eare to the poore, and giue him a friendly answere with meekenesse.
text.NABRE: Give a hearing to the poor, and return their greeting with deference;
text.NRSV: Give a hearing to the poor, and return their greeting politely.
text.DRB: Bow down thine ear to the poor without sadness, and render thy debt, and answer him peaceable words in mildness.
Deliver him that suffereth injury out of the hand of the proud: and be not faint-hearted in thy soul.
text.NABRE: Deliver the oppressed from their oppressors; right judgment should not be repugnant to you.

text.KJV: 4:10 Be as a father vnto the fatherlesse, and in stead of a husband vnto their mother, so shalt thou be as the sonne of the most high, and he shall loue thee more then thy mother doeth.
text.NABRE: Be like a father to orphans, and take the place of a husband to widows. Then God will call you his child, and he will be merciful to you and deliver you from the pit.
text.NRSV: Be like a father to orphans, and like a husband to their mother; you will then be like a son of the Most High, and he will love you more than your mother does.
text.DRB: In judging be merciful to pupils as a father, & as an husband to their mother:

text.KJV: 4:11 Wisedome exalteth her children, and layeth hold of them that seeke her.
text.NABRE: The Rewards of Wisdom Wisdom teaches her children and admonishes all who can understand her.
text.NRSV: Wisdom teaches her children and gives help to those who seek her.
text.DRB: and thou shalt be as the obedient son of the Highest, and he will have mercy on thee more than a mother.

text.KJV: 4:12 He that loueth her, loueth life, and they that seeke to her earely, shall be filled with ioy.
text.NABRE: Those who love her love life; those who seek her out win the Lord ’s favor.
text.NRSV: Whoever loves her loves life, and those who seek her from early morning are filled with joy.
text.DRB: Wisdom inspireth life to her children, and receiveth them that seek after her, and will go before them in the way of justice.
And he that loveth her, loveth life: and they that shall watch to her, shall embrace her sweetness.
text.NABRE: Those who hold her fast will attain glory, and they shall abide in the blessing of the Lord.

text.DRB: They that shall hold her, shall inherit life: and whither-soever he shall enter, God will bless him.
text.NABRE: Those who serve her serve the Holy One; those who love her the Lord loves.

text.DRB: They that serve her, shall be servants to the holy: and them that love her God loveth.
text.NABRE: “Whoever obeys me will judge nations; whoever listens to me will dwell in my inmost chambers.

text.DRB: He that heareth her, shall judge nations: and he that beholdeth her, shall remain confident.
text.NABRE: If they remain faithful, they will possess me; their descendants too will inherit me.

text.KJV: 4:17 For at the first she will walke with him by crooked wayes, and bring feare and dread vpon him, and torment him with her discipline, vntill she may trust his soule, and try him by her Lawes.
text.NABRE: “I will walk with them in disguise, and at first I will test them with trials. Fear and dread I will bring upon them and I will discipline them with my constraints. When their hearts are fully with me,
text.NRSV: For at first she will walk with them on tortuous paths; she will bring fear and dread upon them, and will torment them by her discipline until she trusts them.
text.DRB: If he believe her, he shall inherit her, and her creatures shall be in confirmation:
because in tentation she walketh with him, and first of all she chooseth him.
text.NABRE: then I will set them again on the straight path and reveal my secrets to them.

text.DRB: Fear, and dread, and probation she will bring upon him: and she will torment him in the tribulation of her doctrine, till she try him in her cogitations and credit his soul.
text.NABRE: But if they turn away from me, I will abandon them and deliver them over to robbers.”

text.KJV: 4:20 Obserue the opportunitie, and beware of euill, and be not ashamed when it concerneth thy soule.
text.NABRE: Sincerity and Justice My son, watch for the right time; fear what is evil; do not bring shame upon yourself.
text.NRSV: Watch for the opportune time, and beware of evil, and do not be ashamed to be yourself.
text.DRB: And she will establish him, and make a direct way unto him, and rejoice him,

text.KJV: 4:21 For there is a shame that bringeth sinne, and there is a shame which is glorie and grace.
text.NABRE: There is a shame heavy with guilt, and a shame that brings glory and respect.
text.NRSV: For there is a shame that leads to sin, and there is a shame that is glory and favor.
text.DRB: and will disclose her secrets to him, and will heap upon him as treasures knowledge & understanding of justice.
But if he go amiss, she will forsake him, and deliver him into the hands of his enemy.
text.NABRE: Show no favoritism to your own discredit; let no one intimidate you to your own downfall.

text.DRB: Son observe time, and avoid from evil.
text.NABRE: Do not refrain from speaking at the proper time, and do not hide your wisdom;

text.DRB: For thy soul be not ashamed to say the truth.
text.NABRE: For wisdom becomes known through speech, and knowledge through the tongue’s response.

text.DRB: For there is shame that bringeth sin, & there is shame that bringeth glory and grace.
text.NABRE: Never speak against the truth, but of your own ignorance be ashamed.

text.KJV: 4:26 Bee not ashamed to confesse thy sinnes, and force not the course of the riuer.
text.NABRE: Do not be ashamed to acknowledge your sins, and do not struggle against a rushing stream.
text.NRSV: Do not be ashamed to confess your sins, and do not try to stop the current of a river.
text.DRB: Accept no face against thine own face, nor against thy soul a lie.
Reverence not thy neighbour in his offence:
text.NABRE: Do not abase yourself before a fool; do not refuse to do so before rulers.

text.KJV: 4:28 Striue for the trueth vnto death, and the Lord shall fight for thee.
text.NABRE: Even to the death, fight for what is right, and the Lord will do battle for you.
text.NRSV: Fight to the death for truth, and the Lord God will fight for you.
text.DRB: nor keep in a word in time of salvation. Hide not thy wisdom in the beauty thereof.
For by the tongue wisdom is discerned: and understanding, and knowledge, and doctrine by the word of the wise, and steadfastness in the works of justice.
text.NABRE: Do not be haughty in your speech, or lazy and slack in your deeds.

text.DRB: Do not gainsay the word of truth by any means: and be ashamed of the lie of thine unskillfulness.
text.NABRE: Do not be like a lion at home, or sly and suspicious with your servants.

text.DRB: Be not ashamed to confess thy sins, and submit not thyself to every man for sin.
text.NABRE: Do not let your hand be open to receive, but clenched when it is time to give.

text.DRB: Resist not against the face of the mighty, neither labour against the stream of the river.
For justice contend for thy soul, and unto death strive for justice, and God will overthrow thine enemies for thee.
Be not hasty in thy tongue: and unprofitable and remiss in thy works.
Be not as a lion in thy house, overthrowing them of thy household, and oppressing them that are subject to thee.
Let not thine hand be stretched out to receive, and closed to give.

text.KJV: 5:1 Set not thy heart vpon thy goods, and say not, I haue ynough for my life.
text.NABRE: Chapter 5 Against Presumption Do not rely on your wealth, or say, “I have the power.”
text.NRSV: Do not rely on your wealth, or say, “I have enough.”
text.DRB: Attend not to unjust possessions, and say not: I have sufficient livelihood: for it shall nothing profit in the time of vengeance & affliction.

text.KJV: 5:2 Folow not thine owne minde, and thy strength, to walke in the wayes of thy heart:
text.NABRE: Do not rely on your strength in following the desires of your heart.
text.NRSV: Do not follow your inclination and strength in pursuing the desires of your heart.
text.DRB: Follow not in thy strength the concupiscence of thy heart:
and say not: How mighty am I? And who shall bring me under for my facts? For God revenging will revenge.
text.NABRE: Do not say, “Who can prevail against me?” for the Lord will exact punishment.

text.KJV: 5:4 Say not, I haue sinned, and what harme hath happened vnto mee? for the Lord is long suffering, he wil in no wise let thee goe.
text.NABRE: Do not say, “I have sinned, yet what has happened to me?” for the Lord is slow to anger!
text.NRSV: Do not say, “I sinned, yet what has happened to me?” for the Lord is slow to anger.
text.DRB: Say not: I have sinned, and what sorrowful thing hath chanced to me? For the Highest is a patient rewarder.

text.KJV: 5:5 Concerning propitiation, bee not without feare to adde sinne vnto sinne.
text.NABRE: Do not be so confident of forgiveness that you add sin upon sin.
text.NRSV: Do not be so confident of forgiveness that you add sin to sin.
text.DRB: Of sin forgiven be not without fear, neither add thou sin upon sin.
And say not: The mercy of our Lord is great, he will have mercy on the multitude of my sins.
text.NABRE: Do not say, “His mercy is great; my many sins he will forgive.” For mercy and anger alike are with him; his wrath comes to rest on the wicked.

text.KJV: 5:7 Make no tarying to turne to the Lord, and put not off from day to day: for suddenly shal the wrath of the Lord come foorth, and in thy securitie thou shalt be destroyed, and perish in the day of vengeance.
text.NABRE: Do not delay turning back to the Lord, do not put it off day after day. For suddenly his wrath will come forth; at the time of vengeance, you will perish.
text.NRSV: Do not delay to return to the Lord, and do not postpone it from day to day;
text.DRB: For mercy and wrath quickly approach from God, and his wrath looketh upon sinners.

text.KJV: 5:8 Set not thy heart vpon goods vniustly gotten: for they shall not profit thee in the day of calamitie.
text.NABRE: Do not rely on deceitful wealth, for it will be no help on the day of wrath.
text.NRSV: for suddenly the wrath of the Lord will come upon you, and at the time of punishment you will perish.
text.DRB: Slack not to be converted to our Lord, and defer not from day to day.
For his wrath shall come suddenly: and in the time of vengeance he will destroy thee.
text.NABRE: Use and Abuse of the Tongue Do not winnow in every wind, nor walk in every path.

text.KJV: 5:10 Be stedfast in thy vnderstanding, and let thy word be the same.
text.NABRE: Be steadfast regarding your knowledge, and let your speech be consistent.
text.NRSV: Be steadfast in your understanding, and let your speech be consistent.
text.DRB: Be not careful in unjust riches: for they shall not profit thee in the day of obduction and vengeance.

text.KJV: 5:11 Be swift to heare, and let thy life be sincere, & with patience giue answere.
text.NABRE: Be swift to hear, but slow to answer.
text.NRSV: Be quick to hear, but be deliberate in answering.
text.DRB: Toss not thyself into every wind, and go not into every way: for so every sinner is proved by a double tongue.

text.KJV: 5:12 If thou hast vnderstanding, answer thy neighbour, if not, lay thy hand vpon thy mouth.
text.NABRE: If you can, answer your neighbor; if not, place your hand over your mouth!
text.NRSV: If you know what to say, answer your neighbor; but if not, put your hand over your mouth.
text.DRB: Be steadfast in the way of our Lord, & in the truth of thy understanding, and in knowledge, and let the word of peace and justice accompany thee.

text.KJV: 5:13 Honour and shame is in talke; and the tongue of man is his fall.
text.NABRE: Honor and dishonor through speaking! The tongue can be your downfall.
text.NRSV: Honor and dishonor come from speaking, and the tongue of mortals may be their downfall.
text.DRB: Be mild to hear the word, thou mayst understand, and with wisdom utter thou a true answer.
If thou have understanding, answer thy neighbour: but if not, let thine hand be upon thy mouth, lest thou be taken in an unskillful word, and be confounded.
text.NABRE: Do not be called double-tongued; and with your tongue do not slander a neighbor. For shame has been created for the thief, and sore disgrace for the double-tongued.

text.DRB: Honour and glory in the word of the wise, but the tongue of the unwise is his subversion.
text.NABRE: In little or in much, do not act corruptly;

text.DRB: Be not called a whisperer, and be not taken in thy tongue, & confounded.
For upon a thief is confusion & repentance, and a very evil condemnation upon the double tongued, but to the whisperer hatred, and enmity, and contumely.
Justify thou the little one, and the great alike.
Be not for a friend made an enemy to thy neighbour: for the evil man shall inherit reproach and contumely, and every sinner envious and double tongued.
text.NABRE: Chapter 6 Do not be a foe instead of a friend. A bad name, disgrace, and dishonor you will inherit. Thus the wicked, the double-tongued!

text.DRB: Extol not thyself in the cogitation of thy soul as it were a bull: lest perhaps thy strength be quashed,
text.NABRE: Unruly Passions Do not fall into the grip of your passion, lest like fire it consume your strength.

text.DRB: and it eat thy leaves, and destroy thy fruits, and thou be left as a dry tree in the wilderness.
text.NABRE: It will eat your leaves and destroy your fruits, and you will be left like a dry tree.

text.DRB: For a wicked soul shall destroy him that hath it, and it giveth him to be a joy to his enemies, and shall lead him into the lot of the impious.
text.NABRE: For fierce passion destroys its owner and makes him the sport of his enemies.

text.KJV: 6:5 Sweet language will multiply friends: and a faire speaking tongue will increase kinde greetings.
text.NABRE: True Friendship Pleasant speech multiplies friends, and gracious lips, friendly greetings.
text.NRSV: Pleasant speech multiplies friends, and a gracious tongue multiplies courtesies.
text.DRB: A sweet word multiplieth friends, & appeaseth enemies, and a gracious tongue in a good man aboundeth.

text.KJV: 6:6 Be in peace with many: neuerthelesse haue but one counseller of a thousand.
text.NABRE: Let those who are friendly to you be many, but one in a thousand your confidant.
text.NRSV: Let those who are at peace with you be many, but let your advisers be one in a thousand.
text.DRB: Let there be many at peace with thee, and let one of a thousand be thy counseller.

text.KJV: 6:7 If thou wouldst get a friend, proue him first, and be not hasty to credit him.
text.NABRE: When you gain friends, gain them through testing, and do not be quick to trust them.
text.NRSV: When you gain friends, gain them through testing, and do not trust them hastily.
text.DRB: If thou possess a friend, in tentation possess him, and not easily credit him.

text.KJV: 6:8 For some man is a friend for his owne occasion, and will not abide in the day of thy trouble.
text.NABRE: For there are friends when it suits them, but they will not be around in time of trouble.
text.NRSV: For there are friends who are such when it suits them, but they will not stand by you in time of trouble.
text.DRB: For he is a friend according to his own time, and will not abide in the day of tribulation
And there is a friend that is turned to enmity: and there is a friend that will disclose hatred, and reproaches.
text.NABRE: Another is a friend who turns into an enemy, and tells of the quarrel to your disgrace.

text.DRB: And there is a friend companion at the table, and he will not abide in the day of necessity.
text.NABRE: Others are friends, table companions, but they cannot be found in time of affliction.

text.DRB: Afriend if he continue steadfast, shall be to thee as an equal, and in them of thy household shall deal confidently:
text.NABRE: When things go well, they are your other self, and lord it over your servants.

text.DRB: if he humble himself against thee, and hide himself from thy face, thou shalt have friendship of one accord for good.
text.NABRE: If disaster comes upon you, they turn against you and hide themselves.

text.KJV: 6:13 Separate thy selfe from thine enemies, and take heed of thy friends.
text.NABRE: Stay away from your enemies, and be on guard with your friends.
text.NRSV: Separate yourself from your enemies, and be on the watch where your friends are concerned.
text.DRB: Be separated from thine enemies, & take heed of thy friends.

text.KJV: 6:14 A faithfull friend is a strong defence: and hee that hath found such an one, hath found a treasure.
text.NABRE: Faithful friends are a sturdy shelter; whoever finds one finds a treasure.
text.NRSV: Faithful friends are a sturdy shelter; whoever finds one has found a treasure.
text.DRB: Afaithful friend, is a strong protection: and he that hath found him, hath found a treasure.

text.KJV: 6:15 Nothing doeth counteruaile a faithful friend, and his excellencie is vnualuable.
text.NABRE: Faithful friends are beyond price, no amount can balance their worth.
text.NRSV: Faithful friends are beyond price; no amount can be weighed out against their worth.
text.DRB: To a faithful friend there is no comparison, and there is no poise of gold and silver able to countervail the goodness of his fidelity.

text.KJV: 6:16 A faithfull friend is the medicine of life, and they that feare the Lord shal finde him.
text.NABRE: Faithful friends are life-saving medicine; those who fear God will find them.
text.NRSV: Faithful friends are life-saving medicine; and those who fear the Lord will find them.
text.DRB: Afaithful friend, is the medicine of life & immortality: & they that fear our Lord, shall find him.
He that feareth God, shall likewise have good friendship: because according to him shall his friend be.
text.NABRE: Those who fear the Lord enjoy stable friendship, for as they are, so will their neighbors be.

text.KJV: 6:18 My sonne, gather instruction from thy youth vp: so shalt thou finde wisedome till thine old age.
text.NABRE: Blessings of Wisdom My child, from your youth choose discipline; and when you have gray hair you will find wisdom.
text.NRSV: My child, from your youth choose discipline, and when you have gray hair you will still find wisdom.
text.DRB: Son, from thy youth receive doctrine, and even to thy hoar hairs thou shalt find wisdom.

text.KJV: 6:19 Come vnto her as one that ploweth, and soweth, and wait for her good fruits, for thou shalt not toile much in labouring about her, but thou shalt eat of her fruits right soone.
text.NABRE: As though plowing and sowing, draw close to her; then wait for her bountiful crops. For in cultivating her you will work but little, and soon you will eat her fruit.
text.NRSV: Come to her like one who plows and sows, and wait for her good harvest.
text.DRB: As he that ploweth, and that soweth, go to her, and expect her good fruits.
For in her work thou shalt labour a little, and shalt quickly eat of her generation.
text.NABRE: She is rough ground to the fool! The stupid cannot abide her.

text.DRB: How exceeding sharp is wisdom to the unlearned men, and the unwise will not continue in her.
text.NABRE: She will be like a burdensome stone to them, and they will not delay in casting her aside.

text.DRB: As the virtue of a stone she shall be a probation in them, and they will not stay to cast her forth.
text.NABRE: For discipline is like her name, she is not accessible to many.

text.DRB: For the wisdom of doctrine is according to her name, and she is not manifest to many, but to whom she is known, she continueth even to the sight of God.
text.NABRE: Listen, my child, and take my advice; do not refuse my counsel.

text.DRB: Hear my son, and take counsel of understanding, and cast not away my counsel.
text.NABRE: Put your feet into her fetters, and your neck under her yoke.

text.DRB: Thrust thy foot into her fetters, and thy neck into her chains.
text.NABRE: Bend your shoulders and carry her and do not be irked at her bonds.

text.DRB: Put under thy shoulder, and carry her, and be not weary of her bands.
text.NABRE: With all your soul draw close to her; and with all your strength keep her ways.

text.KJV: 6:27 Search and seeke, and shee shall bee made knowen vnto thee, and when thou hast got hold of her, let her not goe.
text.NABRE: Inquire and search, seek and find; when you get hold of her, do not let her go.
text.NRSV: Search her out and seek her, and she will become known to you; and when you get hold of her, do not let her go.
text.DRB: With all thy mind go to her, and with all thy strength keep her ways.

text.KJV: 6:28 For at the last thou shalt finde her rest, and that shalbe turned to thy ioy.
text.NABRE: Thus at last you will find rest in her, and she will become your joy.
text.NRSV: For at last you will find the rest she gives, and she will be changed into joy for you.
text.DRB: Search her out, and she shall be made manifest to thee, and having obtained her, forsake her not:
For in the later end, thou shalt find rest in her, and she shall be turned unto delectation.
text.NABRE: Her fetters will be a place of strength; her snare, a robe of spun gold.

text.DRB: And her fetters shall be to thee for a protection of strength, and foundation of power, & her chains for a stole of glory:
text.NABRE: Her yoke will be a gold ornament; her bonds, a purple cord.

text.DRB: For the beauty of life is in her, and her bands are a healthful binding.
text.NABRE: You will wear her as a robe of glory, and bear her as a splendid crown.

text.DRB: Thou shalt put on her a stole of glory, and as a crown of gratulation thou shalt set her upon thee.
text.NABRE: If you wish, my son, you can be wise; if you apply yourself, you can be shrewd.

text.DRB: Son, if thou attend to me, thou shalt learn: and if thou wilt apply thy mind, thou shalt be wise.
text.NABRE: If you are willing to listen, you can learn; if you pay attention, you can be instructed.

text.DRB: If thou wilt incline thine ear, thou shalt receive doctrine: and if thou love to hear, thou shalt be wise.
text.NABRE: Stand in the company of the elders; stay close to whoever is wise.

text.DRB: Stand in the multitude of wise ancients, and be joined to their wisdom from thy heart, that thou mayst hear all the narration of God, and the praise may not escape thee.
text.NABRE: Be eager to hear every discourse; let no insightful saying escape you.

text.KJV: 6:36 And if thou seest a man of vnderstanding, get thee betimes vnto him, and let thy foote weare the steps of his doore.
text.NABRE: If you see the intelligent, seek them out; let your feet wear away their doorsteps!
text.NRSV: If you see an intelligent person, rise early to visit him; let your foot wear out his doorstep.
text.DRB: And if thou see a wise man, watch after him, and let thy foot wear the steps of his doors.

text.KJV: 6:37 Let thy minde be vpon the ordinances of the Lord, & meditate continually in his commandements: he shal establish thine heart, and giue thee wisedome at thine owne desire.
text.NABRE: Reflect on the law of the Most High, and let his commandments be your constant study. Then he will enlighten your mind, and make you wise as you desire.
text.NRSV: Reflect on the statutes of the Lord, and meditate at all times on his commandments. It is he who will give insight to your mind, and your desire for wisdom will be granted.
text.DRB: Have thy cogitation in the precepts of God, and in his commandments most of all be daily conversant: and he will give thee heart, and the desire of wisdom shall be given thee.

text.KJV: 7:1 Doe no euill, so shall no harme come vnto thee.
text.NABRE: Chapter 7 Conduct Toward God and Neighbor Do no evil, and evil will not overtake you;
text.NRSV: Do no evil, and evil will never overtake you.
text.DRB: Do not evils, and they shall not apprehend thee.

text.KJV: 7:2 Depart from the vniust, and iniquitie shall turne away from thee.
text.NABRE: avoid wickedness, and it will turn away from you.
text.NRSV: Stay away from wrong, and it will turn away from you.
text.DRB: Depart from the wicked, & evil shall fail from thee.

text.KJV: 7:3 My sonne, sow not vpon the furrowes of vnrighteousnesse, and thou shalt not reape them seuen folde.
text.NABRE: Do not sow in the furrows of injustice, lest you harvest it sevenfold.
text.NRSV: Do not sow in the furrows of injustice, or you may reap a sevenfold harvest.
text.DRB: Son, sow not evils in the furrows of justice, & thou shalt not reap them sevenfold.
Seek not of the Lord chief principality, nor of the king the chair of honour.
text.NABRE: Do not seek from God authority or from the king a place of honor.

text.DRB: Justify not thy-self before God, because he is the knower of the heart: and before the king desire not to seem wise.
text.NABRE: Do not parade your righteousness before the Lord, and before the king do not flaunt your wisdom.

text.KJV: 7:6 Seeke not to be iudge, being not able to take away iniquitie, lest at any time thou feare the person of the mightie, and lay a stumbling blocke in the way of thy vprightnesse.
text.NABRE: Do not seek to become a judge if you do not have the strength to root out crime, Lest you show fear in the presence of the prominent and mar your integrity.
text.NRSV: Do not seek to become a judge, or you may be unable to root out injustice;
text.DRB: Seek not to be made a judge, unless thou be able by power to break iniquities: lest perhaps thou fear the face of the mighty, and put a scandal in thine equity.
Sin not against the multitude of a city, neither thrust thy-self into the people,
text.NABRE: Do not be guilty of any evil before the city court or disgrace yourself before the assembly.

text.KJV: 7:8 Bind not one sinne vpon another, for in one thou shalt not be vnpunished.
text.NABRE: Do not plot to repeat a sin; even for one, you will not go unpunished.
text.NRSV: Do not commit a sin twice, for not even once will you go unpunished.
text.DRB: nor bind together double sins: for neither in one shalt thou be free from punishment.
Be not faint-hearted in thy mind:
text.NABRE: Do not say, “He will appreciate my many gifts; the Most High God will accept my offerings.”

text.KJV: 7:10 Be not faint hearted when thou makest thy prayer, and neglect not to giue almes.
text.NABRE: Do not be impatient in prayer or neglect almsgiving.
text.NRSV: Do not grow weary in prayer; do not neglect to give alms.
text.DRB: despise not to pray, and to give alms.

text.KJV: 7:11 Laugh no man to scorne in the bitternesse of his soule: for there is one which humbleth and exalteth.
text.NABRE: Do not ridicule the embittered; Remember: there is One who exalts and humbles.
text.NRSV: Do not ridicule a person who is embittered in spirit, for there is One who humbles and exalts.
text.DRB: Say not: In the multitude of my gifts God will have respect, and when I offer to God most high, he will receive my gifts.
Laugh not a man to scorn in the bitterness of his soul: for there is that humbleth and exalteth, God the overseer of all.
text.NABRE: Do not plot mischief against your relative or against your friend and companion.

text.DRB: Plow not a lie against thy brother: neither do thou likewise against thy friend.
text.NABRE: Refuse to tell lie after lie, for it never results in good.

text.KJV: 7:14 Use not many words in a multitude of Elders, and make not much babling when thou prayest.
text.NABRE: Do not babble in the assembly of the elders or repeat the words of your prayer.
text.NRSV: Do not prattle in the assembly of the elders, and do not repeat yourself in your prayer.
text.DRB: Be not willing to make any lie: for the custom thereof is not good.

text.KJV: 7:15 Hate not laborious worke, neither husbandrie, which the most High hath ordeined.
text.NABRE: Do not hate hard work; work was assigned by God.
text.NRSV: Do not hate hard-working labor or farm work, which was created by the Most High.
text.DRB: Be not full of words in a multitude of ancients, and iterate not a word in thy speech.
Hate not laborious works, and husbandry created of the Highest.
text.NABRE: Do not esteem yourself more than your compatriots; remember, his wrath will not delay.

text.DRB: Count not thyself in the multitude of men without discipline.
text.NABRE: More and more, humble your pride; what awaits mortals is worms.

text.KJV: 7:18 Change not a friend for any good by no meanes: neither a faithfull brother for the gold of Ophir.
text.NABRE: Duties of Family Life, Religion and Charity Do not barter a friend for money, or a true brother for the gold of Ophir.
text.NRSV: Do not exchange a friend for money, or a real brother for the gold of Ophir.
text.DRB: Be mindful of wrath, because it will not slack.

text.KJV: 7:19 Forgoe not a wise and good woman: for her grace is aboue gold.
text.NABRE: Do not reject a sensible wife; a gracious wife is more precious than pearls.
text.NRSV: Do not miss out on a wise and good wife, for her charm is worth more than gold.
text.DRB: Humble thy spirit very much: because the vengeance of the flesh of the impious, is fire and the worm.
Do not prevaricate against thy friend deferring money, nor despise thy dearest brother for gold.
text.NABRE: Do not mistreat a servant who works faithfully, or laborers who devote themselves to their task.

text.KJV: 7:21 Let thy soule loue a good seruant, and defraud him not of liberty.
text.NABRE: Love wise servants as yourself; do not refuse them freedom.
text.NRSV: Love a wise slave like yourself; do not withhold from him his freedom.
text.DRB: Depart not from a wise and good woman, which thou hast gotten in the fear of our Lord: for the grace of her bashfulness is above gold.
Hurt not the servant that worketh in truth, nor the hired man that giveth his soul.
text.NABRE: Do you have livestock? Look after them; if they are dependable, keep them.

text.KJV: 7:23 Hast thou children? instruct them, and bow downe their necke from their youth.
text.NABRE: Do you have sons? Correct them and cure their stubbornness in their early youth.
text.NRSV: If you have children, discipline them, and make them obedient from their youth.
text.DRB: Let a wise servant be beloved of thee as thy soul, defraud him not of liberty, nor leave him needy.
Hast thou cattle? Look well to them: and if they be profitable, let them continue with thee.
text.NABRE: Do you have daughters? Keep them chaste, and do not be indulgent to them.

text.DRB: Hast thou children? Instruct them, & bow them from their childhood.
text.NABRE: Give your daughter in marriage, and a worry comes to an end; but give her to a sensible man.

text.DRB: Hast thou daughters? Keep their body, and shew not thy countenance merry towards them.
text.NABRE: Do you have a wife? Do not mistreat her, but do not trust the wife you hate.

text.KJV: 7:27 Honour thy father with thy whole heart, and forget not the sorrowes of thy mother.
text.NABRE: With your whole heart honor your father; your mother’s birth pangs do not forget.
text.NRSV: With all your heart honor your father, and do not forget the birth pangs of your mother.
text.DRB: Bestow thy daughter, and thou shalt do a great work, and give her to a wise man.

text.KJV: 7:28 Remember that thou wast begot of them, and how canst thou recompense them the things that they haue done for thee?
text.NABRE: Remember, of these parents you were born; what can you give them for all they gave you?
text.NRSV: Remember that it was of your parents you were born; how can you repay what they have given to you?
text.DRB: If thou have a wife according to thy soul, cast her not off: and to her that is hateful commit not thyself. With thy whole heart
honour thy father, and forget not the groanings of thy mother:
text.NABRE: With all your soul fear God and revere his priests.

text.DRB: remember that thou hadst not been born but by them: and recompense them, as they also thee.
text.NABRE: With all your strength love your Maker and do not neglect his ministers.

text.DRB: In all thy soul fear our Lord, and sanctify his Priests.
text.NABRE: Honor God and respect the priest; give him his portion as you have been commanded: First fruits and contributions, his portion of victims and holy offerings.

text.KJV: 7:32 And stretch thine hand vnto the poore, that thy blessing may be perfected
text.NABRE: To the poor also extend your hand, that your blessing may be complete.
text.NRSV: Stretch out your hand to the poor, so that your blessing may be complete.
text.DRB: With all thy strength love him that made thee: & forsake not his ministers.

text.KJV: 7:33 A gift hath grace in the sight of euery man liuing, and for the dead deteine it not.
text.NABRE: Give your gift to all the living, and do not withhold your kindness from the dead.
text.NRSV: Give graciously to all the living; do not withhold your kindness even from the dead.
text.DRB: Honour God with all thy soul, and honour the Priests, and purge thyself with the arms.

text.KJV: 7:34 Faile not to bee with them that weepe, and mourne with them that mourne.
text.NABRE: Do not avoid those who weep, but mourn with those who mourn.
text.NRSV: Do not avoid those who weep, but mourn with those who mourn.
text.DRB: Give them the portion, as it is commanded thee, of the first fruits and purgation: and of thy negligence purge thyself with few.

text.KJV: 7:35 Be not slow to visit the sicke: for that shall make thee to be beloued.
text.NABRE: Do not hesitate to visit the sick, because for such things you will be loved.
text.NRSV: Do not hesitate to visit the sick, because for such deeds you will be loved.
text.DRB: The gift of thine arms and the sacrifice of sanctification thou shalt offer to our Lord, and the first of holy things:

text.KJV: 7:36 Whatsoeuer thou takest in hand, remember the end, and thou shalt neuer doe amisse.
text.NABRE: In whatever you do, remember your last days, and you will never sin.
text.NRSV: In all you do, remember the end of your life, and then you will never sin.
text.DRB: and to the poor stretch out thine hand, that thy propitiation may be perfected, and thy blessing.
The grace of a gift is in the sight of all the living, and from the dead stay not grace.
Want not in consolation to them that weep, and walk with them that mourn.
Be not loath to visit the sick: for by these things thou shalt be confirmed in love.
In all thy works remember thy later ends, and thou wilt not sin for ever.

text.KJV: 8:1 Striue not with a mighty man, lest thou fall into his hands.
text.NABRE: Chapter 8 Prudence in Dealing with Others Do not contend with the mighty, lest you fall into their power.
text.NRSV: Do not contend with a powerful person, or you may fall into his hands.
text.DRB: Strive not with a mighty man, lest perhaps thou fall into his hands.

text.KJV: 8:2 Bee not at variance with a rich man, lest he ouerweigh thee: for gold hath destroyed many, and peruerted the hearts of kings.
text.NABRE: Do not quarrel with the rich, lest they pay out the price of your downfall. For gold has unsettled many, and wealth perverts the character of princes.
text.NRSV: Do not quarrel with the rich, in case their resources outweigh yours; for gold has ruined many, and has perverted the minds of kings.
text.DRB: Contend not with a rich man, lest perhaps he make an action against thee.

text.KJV: 8:3 Striue not with a man that is full of tongue, and heape not wood vpon his fire.
text.NABRE: Do not quarrel with loud-mouths, or heap wood upon their fire.
text.NRSV: Do not argue with a loud-mouthed person, and do not heap wood on his fire.
text.DRB: For gold and silver hath destroyed many, and hath reached even to the heart of kings, and hath turned them.
Strive not with a man full of tongue, & thou shalt not heap sticks upon his fire.
text.NABRE: Do not associate with the senseless, lest your ancestors be insulted.

text.KJV: 8:5 Reproch not a man that turneth from sinne, but remember that we are all worthy of punishment.
text.NABRE: Do not reproach one who turns away from sin; remember, we all are guilty.
text.NRSV: Do not reproach one who is turning away from sin; remember that we all are deserving of punishment.
text.DRB: Communicate not with the ignorant man, lest he speak ill of thy progeny.

text.KJV: 8:6 Dishonour not a man in his old age: for euen some of vs waxe old.
text.NABRE: Do not insult one who is old, for some of us will also grow old.
text.NRSV: Do not dishonor an old person, for some of us are growing old.
text.DRB: Despise not a man that turneth himself from sin, nor upbraid him therewith: remember that we are all in state to be blamed.

text.KJV: 8:7 Reioice not ouer thy greatest enemie being dead, but remember that we die all.
text.NABRE: Do not rejoice when someone dies; remember, we are all to be gathered in.
text.NRSV: Do not rejoice over anyone’s death; remember that we all must die.
text.DRB: Despise not a man in his old age: for we also shall become old.

text.KJV: 8:8 Despise not the discourse of the wise, but acquaint thy selfe with their prouerbs; for of them thou shalt learne instruction, & how to serue great men with ease.
text.NABRE: Do not neglect the discourse of the wise, but busy yourself with their proverbs; For in this way you will acquire the training to stand in the presence of princes.
text.NRSV: Do not slight the discourse of the sages, but busy yourself with their maxims; because from them you will learn discipline and how to serve princes.
text.DRB: Rejoice not of thine enemy dead: knowing that we do all die, & would not that others should joy thereat.

text.KJV: 8:9 Misse not the discourse of the Elders: for they also learned of their fathers, and of them thou shalt learne vnderstanding, and to giue answere as need requireth.
text.NABRE: Do not reject the tradition of the elders which they have heard from their ancestors; For from it you will learn how to answer when the need arises.
text.NRSV: Do not reject the tradition of the elders, which they learned from their ancestors; for from it you will learn how to understand and how to give an answer in time of need.
text.DRB: Despise not the narration of wise ancients, and in their proverbs be thou conversant.

text.KJV: 8:10 Kindle not the coales of a sinner, lest thou be burnt with the flame of his fire.
text.NABRE: Do not kindle the coals of sinners, lest you be burned in their flaming fire.
text.NRSV: Do not kindle the coals of a sinner, or you may be burned in his flaming fire.
text.DRB: For of them thou shalt learn wisdom, and doctrine of understanding, and to serve great men without blame.
Let not the narration of the ancients escape thee: for they learned of their fathers:
text.NABRE: Do not give ground before scoundrels; it will set them in ambush against you.

text.DRB: because of them thou shalt learn understanding, and in time of necessity to give answer.
text.NABRE: Do not lend to one more powerful than yourself; or if you lend, count it as lost.

text.DRB: Kindle not the coals of sinners rebuking them, and be not kindled with the flame of the fire of their sins.
text.NABRE: Do not give collateral beyond your means; consider any collateral a debt you must pay.

text.DRB: Stand not against the face of a contumelious person, lest he sit as a spy in wait for thy mouth.
text.NABRE: Do not go to court against a judge, for the case will be settled in his favor.

text.KJV: 8:15 Trauaile not by the way with a bold fellow, least he become grieuous vnto thee: for he will doe according to his owne will, and thou shalt perish with him through his folly.
text.NABRE: Do not travel with the ruthless lest they weigh you down with calamity; For they will only go their own way, and through their folly you will also perish.
text.NRSV: Do not travel with a reckless person, or he will be a burden to you; for he will do as he pleases, and through his folly you will perish with him.
text.DRB: Lend not to a man mightier than thyself, and if thou doest lend, count it as lost.
Be not surety above thy power: and if thou be surety, think as if thou were to pay it.
text.NABRE: Do not defy the quick-tempered, or ride with them through the desert. For bloodshed is nothing to them; when there is no one to help, they will destroy you.

text.DRB: Judge not against a judge: because he judgeth according to that which is just.
text.NABRE: Do not take counsel with simpletons, for they cannot keep a confidence.

text.DRB: With the audacious go not on the way, lest perhaps he burden thee with his evils: for he goeth according to his own will, and thou shalt perish together with his folly.
text.NABRE: Before a stranger do nothing that should be kept secret, for you do not know what it will produce later on.

text.DRB: With an angry man make no brawl, and with the audacious go not into the desert: because blood is as nothing before him, and where there is no help, he will overthrow thee.
text.NABRE: Open your heart to no one, do not banish your happiness.

text.DRB: Confer no counsel with fools, for they cannot love but such things as please them.
Before a stranger do no matter of counsel: for thou knowest not what he will bring forth.
Make not thy heart manifest to every man: lest perhaps he repay thee false kindness, and speak reproachfully to thee.

text.KJV: 9:1 Be not iealous ouer the wife of thy bosome, and teach her not an euil lesson against thy selfe.
text.NABRE: Chapter 9 Advice Concerning Women Do not be jealous of the wife of your bosom, lest you teach her to do evil against you.
text.NRSV: Do not be jealous of the wife of your bosom, or you will teach her an evil lesson to your own hurt.
text.DRB: Be not jealous over the wife of thy bosom, lest she shew upon thee the malice of wicked doctrine.

text.KJV: 9:2 Giue not thy soule vnto a woman, to set her foot vpon thy substance.
text.NABRE: Do not give a woman power over you to trample on your dignity.
text.NRSV: Do not give yourself to a woman and let her trample down your strength.
text.DRB: Give not to a woman the power of thy soul, lest she go in thy strength, and thou be confounded.

text.KJV: 9:3 Meete not with an harlot, least thou fall into her snares.
text.NABRE: Do not go near a strange woman, lest you fall into her snares.
text.NRSV: Do not go near a loose woman, or you will fall into her snares.
text.DRB: Look not upon a woman that is desirous of many: lest perhaps thou fall into her snares.
With her that is a dancer be not daily conversant, nor hear her, lest perhaps thou perish in her efficacy.
text.NABRE: Do not dally with a singer, lest you be captivated by her charms.

text.DRB: Behold not a virgin, lest perhaps thou be scandalized in her beauty.
text.NABRE: Do not entertain any thoughts about a virgin, lest you be enmeshed in damages for her.

text.DRB: Give not thy soul to harlots in any point: lest thou destroy thyself, and thine inheritance.
text.NABRE: Do not give yourself to a prostitute lest you lose your inheritance.

text.DRB: Look not round about in the ways of the city, nor wander up and down in the streets thereof.
text.NABRE: Do not look around the streets of the city or wander through its squares.

text.KJV: 9:8 Turne away thine eye from a beautifull woman, and looke not vpon anothers beautie: for many haue beene deceiued by the beautie of a woman, for heerewith loue is kindled as a fire.
text.NABRE: Avert your eyes from a shapely woman; do not gaze upon beauty that is not yours; Through woman’s beauty many have been ruined, for love of it burns like fire.
text.NRSV: Turn away your eyes from a shapely woman, and do not gaze at beauty belonging to another; many have been seduced by a woman’s beauty, and by it passion is kindled like a fire.
text.DRB: Turn away thy face from a trimmed woman, and gaze not about upon another's beauty.
By the beauty of a woman many have perished: and hereby concupiscence is inflamed as a fire.
text.NABRE: Never recline at table with a married woman, or drink intoxicants with her, Lest your heart be drawn to her and you go down in blood to the grave.

text.KJV: 9:10 Forsake not an old friend, for the new is not comparable to him: a new friend is as new wine: when it is old, thou shalt drinke it with pleasure.
text.NABRE: Choice of Friends Do not abandon old friends; new ones cannot equal them. A new friend is like new wine— when it has aged, you drink it with pleasure.
text.NRSV: Do not abandon old friends, for new ones cannot equal them. A new friend is like new wine; when it has aged, you can drink it with pleasure.
text.DRB: Every woman, that is an harlot, shall be trodden upon as dung in the way.

text.KJV: 9:11 Enuy not the glory of a sinner: for thou knowest not what shall be his end.
text.NABRE: Do not envy the wicked for you do not know when their day will come.
text.NRSV: Do not envy the success of sinners, for you do not know what their end will be.
text.DRB: Many having admired the beauty of another man's wife, have become reprobate, for her communication burneth as fire.
Sit not at all with another man's wife, nor repose upon the bed with her:
text.NABRE: Do not delight in the pleasures of the ungodly; remember, they will not die unpunished.

text.KJV: 9:13 Keepe thee farre from the man that hath power to kill, so shalt thou not doubt the feare of death: and if thou come vnto him, make no fault, least he take away thy life presently: remember that thou goest in the midst of snares, and that thou walkest vpon the battlements of the citie.
text.NABRE: Keep away from those who have power to kill, and you will not be filled with the dread of death. But if you come near them, do not offend them, lest they take away your life. Know that you are stepping among snares and walking over a net.
text.NRSV: Keep far from those who have power to kill, and you will not be haunted by the fear of death.
text.DRB: and strive not with her at the wine, lest perhaps thy heart decline toward her, & with thy blood thou fall into perdition.
Forsake not an old friend: for the new will not be like to him.
text.NABRE: As best you can, answer your neighbor, and associate with the wise.

text.KJV: 9:15 Let thy talke be with the wise, and all thy communication in the law of the most high.
text.NABRE: With the learned exchange ideas; and let all your conversation be about the law of the Most High.
text.NRSV: Reason with the intelligent, and let all your argument be about the law of the Most High.
text.DRB: Anew friend, is as new wine: it shall wax old, and thou shalt drink it with sweetness.
Do not zealously desire the glory and the riches of a sinner: for thou knowest not what his subversion shall be.
text.NABRE: Take the righteous for your table companions; and let your glory be in the fear of God.

text.KJV: 9:17 For the hand of the artificer, the worke shall be commended: and the wise ruler of the people, for his speech.
text.NABRE: Concerning Rulers Work by skilled hands will earn praise; but the people’s leader is proved wise by his words.
text.NRSV: A work is praised for the skill of the artisan, so a people’s leader is proved wise by his words.
text.DRB: Let not the injury of the unjust please thee, knowing that even to hell the impious shall not please.
Be far from the man that hath power to kill, and thou shalt not suspect the fear of death.
text.NABRE: Loud mouths are feared in their city, and whoever is reckless in speech is hated.

text.DRB: And if thou come to him, commit nothing, lest perhaps he take away thy life.
Know it to be communication with death; because thou shalt go in the midst of snares, and shalt walk upon the weapons of the sorrowful.
According to thy power beware thee of thy neighbour, and treat with the wise and prudent.
Let just men be thy guests, and let thy gloriation be in the fear of God,
and let the cogitation of God be in thy understanding, & all thine enarration in the precepts of the Highest.
