All iniquity is as a two-edged sword, there is no remedy for the wound thereof.
text.NABRE: Panic and pride wipe out wealth; so too the house of the proud is uprooted.

text.KJV: 21:5 A prayer out of a poore mans mouth reacheth to the eares of God, and his iudgement commeth speedily.
text.NABRE: Prayer from the lips of the poor is heard at once, and justice is quickly granted them.
text.NRSV: The prayer of the poor goes from their lips to the ears of God, and his judgment comes speedily.
text.DRB: Brawling and injuries shall bring the substance to nothing: and the house that is very rich, shall be made nothing by pride: so the substance of the proud shall be rooted out.
The prayer of the poor out of the mouth shall come to his ears, and judgement shall come for him speedily.
text.NABRE: Whoever hates correction walks the sinner’s path, but whoever fears the Lord repents in his heart.

text.DRB: He that hateth chastisement, is the trace of a sinner: and he that feareth God, will turn to his own heart.
text.NABRE: Glib speakers are widely known, but when they slip the sensible perceive it.

text.DRB: The mighty in a bold tongue is known a far off, and a wiseman knoweth himself to fall by him.
text.NABRE: Those who build their houses with someone else’s money are like those who collect stones for their funeral mounds.

text.DRB: He that buildeth his house at other men's charges, is as he that gathereth his stones in the winter.
text.NABRE: A band of criminals is like a bundle of tow; they will end in a flaming fire.

text.KJV: 21:10 The way of sinners is made plaine with stones, but at the ende thereof is the pit of hell.
text.NABRE: The path of sinners is smooth stones, but its end is the pit of Sheol.
text.NRSV: The way of sinners is paved with smooth stones, but at its end is the pit of Hades.
text.DRB: The synagogue of sinners is as tow gathered together, and their consummation a flame of fire.
The way of sinners is paved with stones, & in their end, hell, & darkness, and pains.
text.NABRE: The Wise and Foolish: A Contrast Those who keep the Law control their thoughts; perfect fear of the Lord is wisdom.

text.DRB: He that keepeth justice, shall contain the understanding thereof.
text.NABRE: One who is not clever can never be taught, but there is a cleverness filled with bitterness.

text.DRB: The consummation of the fear of God wisdom and understanding.
text.NABRE: The knowledge of the wise wells up like a flood, and their counsel like a living spring.

text.DRB: He shall not be taught, that is not wise in good.
text.NABRE: A fool’s mind is like a broken jar: it cannot hold any knowledge at all.

text.KJV: 21:15 If a skilfull man heare a wise word, hee will commend it, and adde vnto it: but assoone as one of no vnderstanding heareth it, it displeaseth him, and he casteth it behinde his backe.
text.NABRE: When the intelligent hear a wise saying, they praise it and add to it. The wanton hear it with distaste and cast it behind their back.
text.NRSV: When an intelligent person hears a wise saying, he praises it and adds to it;
text.DRB: But there is wisdom that aboundeth in evil: and there is no understanding where bitterness is.

text.KJV: 21:16 The talking of a foole is like a burden in the way: but grace shall be found in the lips of the wise.
text.NABRE: A fool’s chatter is like a load on a journey, but delight is to be found on the lips of the intelligent.
text.NRSV: The chatter of a fool is like a burden on a journey, but delight is found in the speech of the intelligent.
text.DRB: The knowledge of the wise shall abound as an inundation, and his counsel is permanent as a fountain of life.
The heart of a fool is as a broken vessel, and all wisdom it shall not hold.
text.NABRE: The views of the prudent are sought in an assembly, and their words are taken to heart.

text.DRB: Aman of knowledge will praise what soever wise word he shall hear, and will apply it to himself: the riotous man hath heard it, and it shall displease him, and he will cast it behind his back.
text.NABRE: Like a house in ruins is wisdom to a fool; to the stupid, knowledge is incomprehensible chatter.

text.DRB: The narration of a fool is as a burden in the way: for in the lips of the wise shall grace be found.
text.NABRE: To the senseless, education is fetters on the feet, like manacles on the right hand.

text.KJV: 21:20 A foole lifteth vp his voyce with laughter, but a wise man doeth scarce smile a litle.
text.NABRE: Fools raise their voice in laughter, but the prudent at most smile quietly.
text.NRSV: A fool raises his voice when he laughs, but a clever person smiles quietly.
text.DRB: The mouth of the prudent is sought in the Church, and they will think upon his words in their hearts.
As a house destroyed, so is wisdom to a fool: & the knowledge of the unwise inexplicable words.
text.NABRE: Like a gold ornament is education to the wise, like a bracelet on the right arm.

text.DRB: Fetters on the feet, doctrine to a fool, and as manacles upon the right hand.
text.NABRE: A fool steps boldly into a house, while the well-bred are slow to make an entrance.

text.DRB: Afool in laughter exalteth his voice, but a wise man will scarce laugh secretly.
text.NABRE: A boor peeps through the doorway of a house, but the educated stay outside.

text.DRB: Doctrine to the prudent is a golden ornament, and as it were a bracelet on the right arm.
text.NABRE: It is rude for one to listen at a door; the discreet person would be overwhelmed by the disgrace.

text.DRB: The foot of a fool goeth easily into his neighbour's house: & a cunning man will be abashed at the person of the mighty.
text.NABRE: The lips of the arrogant talk of what is not their concern, but the discreet carefully weigh their words.

text.KJV: 21:26 The heart of fooles is in their mouth, but the mouth of the wise is in their heart.
text.NABRE: The mind of fools is in their mouths, but the mouth of the wise is in their mind.
text.NRSV: The heart of fools is in their mouths, but the mouth of the wise is in their heart.
text.DRB: Afool will look from the window into the house: but he nurtured will stand without.
It is the folly of a man to harken by the door: and a wiseman will be grieved with the contumely.
text.NABRE: When the godless curse their adversary, they really curse themselves.

text.DRB: The lips of the unwise shall tell foolish things: but the words of the wise shall be pondered in balance.
text.NABRE: Slanderers sully themselves, and are hated by their neighbors.

text.DRB: The heart of fools is in their mouth: and the mouth of wisemen is in their heart.
Whilst the impious curseth the devil, he curseth his own soul.
The whisperer shall defile his soul, and shall be hated in all: and he that shall abide with him, shall be odious: the still man and wise shall be honoured.

text.KJV: 22:1 A slouthful man is compared to a filthy stone, and euery one will hisse him out to his disgrace.
text.NABRE: Chapter 22 On Laziness and Foolishness The sluggard is like a filthy stone; everyone hisses at his disgrace.
text.NRSV: The sluggard is like a filthy stone, and everyone hisses at his disgrace.
text.DRB: The sluggard is stoned with a dirty stone, & all men will speak of his disgrace.
The sluggard is stoned with the dung of oxen: and every one that shall touch him, will shake his hands.
text.NABRE: The sluggard is like a lump of dung; whoever touches it shakes it off the hands.

text.KJV: 22:3 An euill nurtured sonne is the dishonour of his father that begate him: and a daughter is borne to his losse.
text.NABRE: An undisciplined child is a disgrace to its father; if it be a daughter, she brings him to poverty.
text.NRSV: It is a disgrace to be the father of an undisciplined son, and the birth of a daughter is a loss.
text.DRB: The confusion of the father is of a son without discipline: and the daughter shall be made of less account.

text.KJV: 22:4 A wise daughter shall bring an inheritance to her husband: but shee that liueth dishonestly, is her fathers heauinesse.
text.NABRE: A thoughtful daughter obtains a husband of her own; a shameless one is her father’s grief.
text.NRSV: A sensible daughter obtains a husband of her own, but one who acts shamefully is a grief to her father.
text.DRB: Awise daughter is an inheritance to her husband: for she that confoundeth, is made a contumely to her father.
She that is bold shameth father and husband, and shall not be inferior to the impious: but of them both she shall be dishonoured.
text.NABRE: A hussy shames her father and her husband; she is despised by both.

text.DRB: Music in mourning is a tale out of time: scourges and doctrine are at all time wisdom.
text.NABRE: Like music at the time of mourning is ill-timed talk, but lashes and discipline are at all times wisdom.

text.KJV: 22:7 Who so teacheth a foole, is as one that gleweth a potsheard together, and as hee that waketh one from a sound sleepe.
text.NRSV: To teach a fool is like gluing a broken pot, or like waking a sleeper from a deep slumber.
text.DRB: He that teacheth a fool, is as he that glueth together a pot-sherd.
He that telleth a word to him that heareth not, is as he that raiseth up a man sleeping out of an heavy sleep.
He speaketh with him that sleepeth, which uttereth wisdom to a fool: and in the end of the narration he saith: Who is this?
Weep upon the dead, for his light hath failed: and weep upon a fool, for he faileth in understanding.

text.KJV: 22:11 Weepe for the dead, for hee hath lost the light: and weepe for the foole, for he wanteth vnderstanding: make litle weeping for the dead, for hee is at rest: but the life of the foole is worse then death.
text.NRSV: Weep for the dead, for he has left the light behind; and weep for the fool, for he has left intelligence behind.
text.DRB: Weep a little upon the dead, because he is at rest.

text.KJV: 22:12 Seuen dayes doe men mourne for him that is dead; but for a foole, and an vngodly man, all the dayes of his life.
text.NRSV: Mourning for the dead lasts seven days, but for a fool or an ungodly person it lasts all the days of their lives.
text.DRB: For the wicked life of the very wicked, above the death of a fool.
The mourning of the dead is seven days: but of a fool and of the impious, all the days of their life.
Speak not much with a fool, and go not with the unwise.
Keep thyself from him, that thou have no molestation, and thou shalt not be defiled with his sin.
Turn aside from him, and thou shalt find rest, and shall not be wearied with his folly.
What shall be heavier than lead? And what other name hath it but fool?
It is easier to bear sand and salt, and a mass of iron, than an unwise man, and a fool, and impious.
Aframe of wood bound together in the foundation of a building, shall not be dissolved: so also the heart confirmed in the cogitation of counsel.
The cogitation of the wise at all time, yea by fear shall not be depraved.
As stakes in high places, and plasterings laid without cost, shall not abide against the face of the wind:

text.KJV: 22:22 If thou hast opened thy mouth against thy friend, feare not, for there may be a reconciliation: except for vpbraiding, or pride, or disclosing of secrets, or a treacherous wound, for, for these things euery friend will depart.
text.NRSV: If you have opened your mouth against a friend, do not worry, for reconciliation is possible. But as for reviling, arrogance, disclosure of secrets, or a treacherous blow—in these cases any friend will flee.
text.DRB: so also a fearful heart in the cogitation of a fool shall not resist against the violence of fear.
As a trembling heart in the cogitation of a fool, all time will not fear, so also he that continueth always in the precepts of God.
He that pricketh the eye, bringeth forth tears: and he that pricketh the heart, bringeth forth feeling.
He that casteth a stone at fowls, and shall throw them down: so he that speaketh reproachfully to his friend, dissolveth friendship.
Although thou drawest a sword at a friend, despair not: for there is returning to a friend.

text.KJV: 22:27 Who shall set a watch before my mouth, and a seale of wisedome vpon my lippes, that I fall not suddenly by them, & that my tongue destroy me not?
text.NRSV: Who will set a guard over my mouth, and an effective seal upon my lips, so that I may not fall because of them, and my tongue may not destroy me?
text.DRB: If thou open a sad mouth, fear not: for there is agreement: except taunt, and reproach, and pride, and revealing of secret, and a treacherous wound: in all these things a friend will fly away.
Possess fidelity with a friend in his poverty, that in his goods also thou mayst rejoice.
In the time of his tribulation continue faithful to him, that in his inheritance also thou mayst be heir with him.
Before the fire the vapour of the chimney, and the smoke of the fire riseth on high: so also before blood evil words, and contumelies, & threats.
I will not be ashamed to salute a friend, from his face I will not hide myself: and if there chance evils to me by him, I will bear it.
Every one that shall hear, will beware of him.
Who will give a guard to my mouth, and a sure seal upon my lips, that I fall not by them, and my tongue destroy me?

text.KJV: 23:1 O Lord, father and gouernour of all my whole life, leaue me not to their counsels, and let me not fall by them.
text.NABRE: Chapter 23 Lord, Father and Master of my life, do not abandon me to their designs, do not let me fall because of them!
text.NRSV: O Lord, Father and Master of my life, do not abandon me to their designs, and do not let me fall because of them!
text.DRB: O Lord father, and dominatour of my life, leave me not in their counsel: nor suffer me to fall in them.
Who layeth on stripes in my cogitation, and in my heart the doctrine of wisdom, and in their ignorances they spare me not, and their offences appear not,
text.NABRE: Who will apply the lash to my thoughts, and to my mind the rod of discipline, That my failings may not be spared or the sins of my heart overlooked?

text.DRB: and my ignorances increase not, and my offences be multiplied, and my sins abound, and I fall in the sight of mine adversaries, & mine enemy rejoice?
text.NABRE: Otherwise my failings may increase, and my sins be multiplied; And I fall before my adversaries, and my enemy rejoice over me?

text.DRB: O Lord father, and God of my life, leave me not in their cogitation.
text.NABRE: Lord, Father and God of my life, do not give me haughty eyes;

text.DRB: Haughtiness of mine eyes give me not, and all desire turn away from me.
text.NABRE: remove evil desire from my heart.

text.DRB: Take from me the concupiscences of the belly, and let not the concupiscences of copulation take hold of me, and give me not over to a shameless and foolish mind.
text.NABRE: Let neither gluttony nor lust overcome me; do not give me up to shameless desires.

text.KJV: 23:7 Heare, O yee children, the discipline of the mouth: He that keepeth it, shall neuer be taken in his lippes.
text.NABRE: Proper Use of the Tongue Listen, my children, to instruction concerning the mouth, for whoever keeps it will not be ensnared.
text.NRSV: Listen, my children, to instruction about the mouth; the one who keeps it will not be caught.
text.DRB: O children hear the doctrine of the mouth: and he that will keep it, shall not perish by his lips, nor be scandalized in most wicked works.
Asinner is taught in his vanity, and the proud and the evil speaker shall be scandalized in them.
text.NABRE: Through the lips the sinner is caught; by them the reviler and the arrogant are tripped up.

text.KJV: 23:9 Accustome not thy mouth to swearing: neither vse thy selfe to the naming of the holy one.
text.NABRE: Do not accustom your mouth to oaths, or habitually utter the Holy Name.
text.NRSV: Do not accustom your mouth to oaths, nor habitually utter the name of the Holy One.
text.DRB: Let not thy mouth be accustomed to swearing: for there be many falls in it.
But let not the naming of God be usual in thy mouth, and meddle not with the names of Saints, because thou shalt not scape free from them.
text.NABRE: Just as a servant constantly under scrutiny will not be without bruises, So one who swears continually by the Holy Name will never remain free from sin.

text.DRB: For as a servant daily examined, lacketh not the mark thereof: so every one that sweareth, and nameth, shall not be wholly purged from sin.
text.NABRE: Those who swear many oaths heap up offenses; and the scourge will never be far from their houses. If they swear in error, guilt is incurred; if they neglect their obligation, the sin is doubly great. If they swear without reason they cannot be declared innocent, for their households will be filled with calamities.

text.DRB: Aman that sweareth much shall be filled with iniquity, and plague shall not depart from his house.
text.NABRE: There are words comparable to death; may they never be heard in the inheritance of Jacob. To the devout all such words are foreign; they do not wallow in sin.

text.DRB: And if he frustrate it, his sin shall be upon him: and if he dissemble, he offendeth double:
text.NABRE: Do not accustom your mouth to coarse talk, for it involves sinful speech.

text.DRB: and if he swear in vain, he shall not be justified: for his house shall be filled with retribution.
text.NABRE: Keep your father and mother in mind when you sit among the mighty, Lest you forget yourself in their presence and disgrace your upbringing. Then you will wish you had never been born and will curse the day of your birth.

text.DRB: There is another contrary speech, also to death, be it not found in the inheritance of Jacob.
text.NABRE: Those accustomed to using abusive language will never acquire discipline as long as they live.

text.DRB: For of the merciful all those things shall be taken away, and they will not wallow in sins.
text.NABRE: Sins of the Flesh Two types of people multiply sins, and a third draws down wrath: Burning passion is like a blazing fire, not to be quenched till it burns itself out; One unchaste with his kindred never stops until fire breaks forth.

text.DRB: Let not thy mouth be accustomed to unuttered speech: for there is in it a word of sin.
text.NABRE: To the unchaste all bread is sweet; he is never through till he dies.

text.KJV: 23:18 A man that breaketh wedlocke, saying thus in his heart, Who seeth me? I am compassed about with darknesse: the walles couer me; & no body seeth me, what neede I to feare? The most high wil not remember my sinnes:
text.NABRE: The man who dishonors his marriage bed says to himself, “Who can see me? Darkness surrounds me, walls hide me, no one sees me. Who can stop me from sinning?” He is not mindful of the Most High,
text.NRSV: A man who violates his marriage bed says to himself, “Who can see me? Darkness surrounds me, the walls hide me, and no one sees me; why should I fear? The Most High will not remember my sins.”
text.DRB: Remember thy father and thy mother, for thou sittest in the midst of great men:

text.KJV: 23:19 Such a man only feareth the eies of men, and knoweth not that the eies of the Lord are tenne thousand times brighter then the Sunne, beholding all the waies of men, and considering the most secret parts.
text.NABRE: fearing only human eyes. He does not realize that the eyes of the Lord, ten thousand times brighter than the sun, Observe every step taken and peer into hidden corners.
text.NRSV: His fear is confined to human eyes, and he does not realize that the eyes of the Lord are ten thousand times brighter than the sun.
text.DRB: lest perhaps God forget thee in their sight, & being besotted with thy daily custom, thou suffer reproach, & hadst better not have been born, and curse the day of thy nativity.
Aman accustomed to the words of reproach, will not be instructed in all his days.
text.NABRE: The one who knows all things before they exist still knows them all after they are made.

text.DRB: Two sorts abound in sins, and the third bringeth wrath and perdition.
text.NABRE: Such a man will be denounced in the streets of the city; and where he least suspects it, he will be apprehended.

text.KJV: 23:22 Thus shall it goe also with the wife, that leaueth her husband, and bringeth in an heire by another:
text.NABRE: So it is with the woman unfaithful to her husband, who offers him an heir by another man.
text.NRSV: So it is with a woman who leaves her husband and presents him with an heir by another man.
text.DRB: An hot soul as a burning fire will not be quenched, till it swallow somewhat.
and a wicked man in the mouth of his flesh will not cease till he kindle a fire.
text.NABRE: First of all, she has disobeyed the law of the Most High; second, she has wronged her husband; Third, through her wanton adultery she has brought forth children by another man.

text.DRB: To a man that is a fornicator all bread is sweet, he will not be weary transgressing unto the end.
text.NABRE: Such a woman will be dragged before the assembly, and her punishment will extend to her children.

text.DRB: Every man that passeth beyond his own bed, contemning against his own soul, and saying: Who seeth me?
text.NABRE: Her children will not take root; her branches will not bring forth fruit.

text.DRB: Darkness compasseth me, and the walls cover me, and no man beholdeth me: whom do I fear? The Highest will not be mindful of my sins.
text.NABRE: She will leave behind an accursed memory; her disgrace will never be blotted out.

text.KJV: 23:27 And they that remaine, shall know that there is nothing better then the feare of the Lord, and that there is nothing sweeter then to take heed vnto the Commandement of the Lord.
text.NABRE: Thus all who dwell on the earth shall know, all who remain in the world shall understand, That nothing is better than the fear of the Lord, nothing sweeter than obeying the commandments of the Lord.
text.NRSV: Those who survive will know that nothing is better than the fear of the Lord, and nothing sweeter than to heed the Lord’s commandments.
text.DRB: And he understandeth not that his eye seeth all things, for that such fear of man expelleth from him the fear of God, & the eyes of men fearing him:

and he knoweth not that the eyes of our Lord are much more brighter than the sun, beholding round about all the ways of man, and the bottom of the depth, and the hearts of men, looking into the hidden parts.
For all things were known unto our Lord God, before they were created: so also after it is perfected he beholdeth all things.
Revenge shall be taken on this man in the streets of the city, and as an horse-colt he shall be chased: and where he expected not, he shall be apprehended.
And he shall be in dishonour with all men, for that he understood not the fear of our Lord.
So every woman also that forsaketh her husband, & getteth inheritance by marriage of another.
For first she hath been unfaithful in the law of the Highest: and secondly she hath sinned against her husband: thirdly she hath fornicated in adultery, and hath gotten her children of another man.
This woman shall be brought into the Church, and upon her children there shall be examination.
Her children shall not take root, & her boughs shall not yield fruit.
She shall leave her memory to be cursed, and her dishonour shall not be wiped out.
And they that are left shall know, that nothing is better than the fear of God: and nothing sweeter, than to have regard to the commandments of our Lord.
It is great glory to follow our Lord: for length of days shall be taken of him.

text.KJV: 24:1 Wisedome shall praise her selfe, and shall glory in the midst of her people.
text.NABRE: Chapter 24 Praise of Wisdom Wisdom sings her own praises, among her own people she proclaims her glory.
text.NRSV: Wisdom praises herself, and tells of her glory in the midst of her people.
text.DRB: Wisdom shall praise her soul, & shall be honoured in God, and shall glory in the midst of her people,

text.KJV: 24:2 In the Congregation of the most high, shall she open her mouth, and triumph before his power.
text.NABRE: In the assembly of the Most High she opens her mouth, in the presence of his host she tells of her glory:
text.NRSV: In the assembly of the Most High she opens her mouth, and in the presence of his hosts she tells of her glory:
text.DRB: and shall open her mouth in the churches of the Highest, and shall glory in the sight of his power,

text.KJV: 24:3 I came out of the mouth of the most High, and couered the earth as a cloud.
text.NABRE: “From the mouth of the Most High I came forth, and covered the earth like a mist.
text.NRSV: “I came forth from the mouth of the Most High, and covered the earth like a mist.”
text.DRB: and in the midst of her people she shall be exalted, and in the holy assembly she shall be admired,
and in the multitude of the elect she shall have praise, and among the blessed she shall be blessed, saying:
text.NABRE: In the heights of heaven I dwelt, and my throne was in a pillar of cloud.

text.DRB: I came forth from the mouth of the Highest, the first begotten before all creatures.
text.NABRE: The vault of heaven I compassed alone, and walked through the deep abyss.

text.DRB: I made that in the heavens, there should rise light that faileth not, and as a cloud I covered all the earth.
text.NABRE: Over waves of the sea, over all the land, over every people and nation I held sway.

text.DRB: I dwelt in the highest places, and my throne is in the pillar of a cloud.
text.NABRE: Among all these I sought a resting place. In whose inheritance should I abide?

text.KJV: 24:8 So the creatour of all things gaue mee a commandement, and hee that made me, caused my tabernacle to rest: and said, Let thy dwelling be in Iacob, and thine inheritance in Israel.
text.NABRE: “Then the Creator of all gave me his command, and my Creator chose the spot for my tent. He said, ‘In Jacob make your dwelling, in Israel your inheritance.’
text.NRSV: “Then the Creator of all things gave me a command, and my Creator chose the place for my tent. He said, ‘Make your dwelling in Jacob, and in Israel receive your inheritance.’”
text.DRB: I alone have gone round about the compass of heaven, and have penetrated into the bottom of the depth, and have walked in the waves of the sea,

text.KJV: 24:9 Hee created mee from the beginning before the world, and I shall neuer faile.
text.NABRE: Before all ages, from the beginning, he created me, and through all ages I shall not cease to be.
text.NRSV: “Before the ages, in the beginning, he created me, and for all the ages I shall not cease to be.”
text.DRB: and stood in all the earth: and in all people.
and in every nation I have had the primacy:
text.NABRE: In the holy tent I ministered before him, and so I was established in Zion.

text.DRB: and I have by strength trodden down the hearts of all the excellent, and the base; and in all these things I sought rest, & I shall abide in the inheritance of our Lord.
text.NABRE: In the city he loves as he loves me, he gave me rest; in Jerusalem, my domain.

text.DRB: Then the creator of all commanded, and said to me: & he that created me, rested in my tabernacle,
text.NABRE: I struck root among the glorious people, in the portion of the Lord, his heritage.

text.DRB: and he said to me: Inhabit in Jacob, and inherit in Israel, and take root in mine elect.
text.NABRE: “Like a cedar in Lebanon I grew tall, like a cypress on Mount Hermon;

text.DRB: From the beginning and before the worlds was I created, and unto the world to come I shall not cease, and in the holy habitation I have ministered before him.
text.NABRE: I grew tall like a palm tree in Engedi, like rosebushes in Jericho; Like a fair olive tree in the field, like a plane tree beside water I grew tall.

text.DRB: And so in Sion was I established, and in the sanctified city likewise I rested, and my power was in Jerusalem.
text.NABRE: Like cinnamon and fragrant cane, like precious myrrh I gave forth perfume; Like galbanum and onycha and mastic, like the odor of incense in the holy tent.

text.DRB: And I took root in an honourable people, and in the portion of my God his inheritance, and my abiding is in the full assembly of saints.
text.NABRE: “I spread out my branches like a terebinth, my branches so glorious and so graceful.

text.DRB: I am exalted as a cedar in Libanus, and as a cypress tree in mount Sion.
text.NABRE: I bud forth delights like a vine; my blossoms are glorious and rich fruit.

text.DRB: As a palm tree in Cades am I exalted, and as a rose plant in Jericho:

text.KJV: 24:19 Come vnto me all ye that be desirous of mee, and fill your selues with my fruits.
text.NRSV: “Come to me, you who desire me, and eat your fill of my fruits.”
text.DRB: As a fair olive tree in the fields, and as a plane tree by the water in the streets am I exalted.

text.KJV: 24:20 For my memorial is sweeter then hony, and mine inheritance then the hony combe.
text.NRSV: “For the memory of me is sweeter than honey, and the possession of me sweeter than the honeycomb.”
text.DRB: I gave an odour as cinnamon, & aromatical balm: as chosen myrrh have I given the sweetness of odour:

text.KJV: 24:21 They that eate mee shall yet be hungry, and they that drinke me shall yet be thirstie.
text.NRSV: “Those who eat of me will hunger for more, and those who drink of me will thirst for more.”
text.DRB: and as storax, and galbanum, and onyx, and aloes, and as Libanus not cut, have I perfumed mine habitation, and mine odour is as balm not mingled.
I have spread out my boughs as the terebinth, and my boughs are of honour & grace.

text.KJV: 24:23 All these things are the booke of the Couenant of the most high God, euen the Law which Moses commanded for an heritage vnto the Congregations of Iacob.
text.NRSV: All this is the book of the covenant of the Most High God, the law that Moses commanded us as an inheritance for the congregations of Jacob.
text.DRB: I as a vine have fructified sweetness of odour: and my flowers are fruit of honour and honesty.
I am the mother of beautiful love, and of fear, and of knowledge, and of holy hope.
In me is all grace of way and truth, in me all hope of life and virtue.
Pass to me all ye that desire me, and be filled of my generations.
For my spirit is sweet above honey, and mine inheritance above honey and the honey comb.
My memory is unto generations of worlds.
They that eat me, shall yet hunger: and they that drink me, shall yet thirst.
He that heareth me, shall not be confounded: and they that work in me, shall not sin.
They that explicate me, shall have life everlasting.
All these things are the book of life, and the testament of the Highest, & the knowledge of truth.
Moyses commanded a law in the precepts of justices, and an inheritance to the house of Jacob, and the promises to Israel.
He appointed to David his servant for to raise up a king of him most strong, and sitting in the throne of honour for ever.
Who filleth wisdom as Phison, and as Tigris in the days of new fruits.
Who replenisheth understanding as Euphrates, who multiplieth it as Jordan in the time of harvest.
Who sendeth discipline as the light, and assisting as Gehon in the day of vintage.
Who first hath perfect knowledge of it, & a weaker shall not search it out.
For her cogitation shall abound above the sea, and her counsels above the great depth.
I wisdom have poured out rivers.
I as a sluse of a mighty water out of the river, I as the river Dioryx, & as a water conduit I came out of paradise.
Isaid: I will water my garden of plants, and will inebriate the fruit of my meadow.
And behold my sluse was made aboundant, and my river came near to a sea.
Because I illuminated doctrine to all as the morning light, & I will declare it far.
I will penetrate all the inferior parts of the earth, and will behold all that sleep, and will illuminate all that hope in our Lord.
I will yet pour out doctrine as prophecy, and will leave it to them that seek wisdom, and will not cease unto their progenies even to the holy age.
See ye that I have not laboured for myself only, but for all that seek out the truth.

text.KJV: 25:1 In three things I was beautified, and stoode vp beautiful, both before God and men: the vnitie of brethren, the loue of neighbours, a man and a wife that agree together.
text.NABRE: Chapter 25 Those Who Are Worthy of Praise With three things I am delighted, for they are pleasing to the Lord and to human beings: Harmony among relatives, friendship among neighbors, and a wife and a husband living happily together.
text.NRSV: I take pleasure in three things, and they are beautiful in the sight of God and of mortals: agreement among brothers and sisters, friendship among neighbors, and a wife and a husband who live in harmony.
text.DRB: In three things my spirit is pleased, which are approved before God, and men:

text.KJV: 25:2 Three sorts of men my soule hateth, and I am greatly offended at their life: a poore man that is proud, a rich man that is a lyar, and an olde adulterer that doteth.
text.NABRE: Three kinds of people I hate, and I loathe their manner of life: A proud pauper, a rich liar, and a lecherous old fool.
text.NRSV: I detest three kinds of people... a beggar who is proud, a rich man who is a liar, and an adulterous old man who lacks good sense.
text.DRB: The concord of brethren, and the love of neighbours, and man and wife well agreeing together.
Three sorts my soul hateth, and I am greatly grieved at their life:
text.NABRE: In your youth you did not gather. How will you find anything in your old age?

text.DRB: A poor man proud: & a rich man a liar: an old man a fool, and doting.
text.NABRE: How appropriate is sound judgment in the gray-haired, and good counsel in the elderly!

text.DRB: The things that thou hast not gathered in thy youth, how shalt thou find them in thy old age?
text.NABRE: How appropriate is wisdom in the aged, understanding and counsel in the venerable!

text.DRB: How beautiful is judgement for a gray head, and for ancients to know counsel!
text.NABRE: The crown of the elderly, wide experience; their glory, the fear of the Lord.

text.KJV: 25:7 There be nine things which I haue iudged in mine heart to be happy, and the tenth I will vtter with my tongue: a man that hath ioy of his children, and he that liueth to see the fall of his enemie.
text.NABRE: There are nine who come to mind as blessed, a tenth whom my tongue proclaims: The man who finds joy in his children, and the one who lives to see the downfall of his enemies.
text.NRSV: I can think of nine whom I would call blessed, and a tenth my tongue proclaims: a man who can rejoice in his children; a man who lives to see the downfall of his enemies.
text.DRB: How beautiful is wisdom for the aged, & understanding glorious, and counsel!

text.KJV: 25:8 Well is him that dwelleth with a wife of vnderstanding, and that hath not slipped with his tongue, and that hath not serued a man more vnworthy then himselfe.
text.NABRE: Happy the man who lives with a sensible woman, and the one who does not plow with an ox and a donkey combined. Happy the one who does not sin with the tongue, who does not serve an inferior.
text.NRSV: Happy is the one who lives with a sensible wife, and the one who does not plow with ox and ass together. Happy is the one who does not slip with the tongue, and the one who has not served a man inferior to himself.
text.DRB: Much cunning is the crown of old men, and the fear of God is their glory.

text.KJV: 25:9 Well is him that hath found prudence, and he that speaketh in the eares of him that will heare.
text.NABRE: Happy the one who finds a friend, who speaks to attentive ears.
text.NRSV: Happy is the one who finds a friend, and the one who speaks to attentive listeners.
text.DRB: Nine things not to be imagined of the heart have I magnified, and the tenth I will tell unto men with my tongue:

text.KJV: 25:10 Oh how great is he that findeth wisedome! yet is there none aboue him that feareth the Lord.
text.NABRE: How great is the one who finds wisdom, but none is greater than the one who fears the Lord.
text.NRSV: How great is the one who finds wisdom! But none is superior to the one who fears the Lord.
text.DRB: Aman that hath joy in his children; living and seeing the subversion of his enemies.
Blessed is he that dwelleth with a wise woman; & that hath not offended with his tongue; and that hath not served such as are unworthy of him.
text.NABRE: Fear of the Lord surpasses all else. To whom can we compare the one who has it?

text.DRB: Blessed is he that findeth a true friend; and that declareth justice to an ear that heareth.
How great is he, that findeth wisdom and knowledge; but he is not above him that feareth our Lord.
The fear of God hath set it-self above all things:
blessed is the man, to whom is given to have the fear of God: he that holdeth it, to whom shall he be resembled?
The fear of God is the beginning of his love: and the beginning of faith is to be fast joined unto it.
The heaviness of the heart is all plague: & all malice, the wickedness of a woman.
And he will see all plague, and not the plague of the heart:
& all wickedness, & not the wickedness of a woman:
and all obduction and not the obduction of them that hate him:
and all revenge, and not the revenge of the enemies.
There is no head worse than the head of a serpent:
and there is no anger above the anger of a woman. It shall be more pleasant to abide with a lion and dragon, than to dwell with a wicked woman.

text.KJV: 25:24 Of the woman came the beginning of sinne, & through her wee all die.
text.NRSV: From a woman sin had its beginning, and because of her we all die.
text.DRB: The wickedness of a woman changeth her face: and darkeneth her countenance as a bear: and will shew it as a sack. In the midst of her neighbours,
her husband groaned, and hearing he sighed a little.
All malice is short to the malice of a woman, the lot of sinners fall upon her.
As the going up a gravelly way in the feet of the aged, so a woman full of tongue to a quiet man.
Look not upon a woman's beauty, and desire not a woman for beauty.
Awoman's anger, and impudency, and confusion is great.
Awoman if she have superiority, is contrary to her husband.
An humbled heart, and heavy countenance, and plague of heart, is a wicked woman.
Feeble hands, and disjointed knees, a woman that doth not make her husband happy.
From woman came the beginning of sin, and by her we do all die.
Give not issue to thy water, no not a little: nor to a wicked woman leave to go forth.
If she walk not at thine hand, she will confound thee in the sight of thine enemies.
Cut her off from thy flesh, lest she always abuse thee.

text.KJV: 26:1 Blessed is the man that hath a vertuous wife, for the number of his dayes shall be double.
text.NABRE: Chapter 26 Happy the husband of a good wife; the number of his days will be doubled.
text.NRSV: Happy is the husband of a good wife; the number of his days will be doubled.
text.DRB: The husband of a good wife is happy: for the number of his years is double.

text.KJV: 26:2 A vertuous woman reioyceth her husband, and he shall fulfill the yeeres of his life in peace.
text.NABRE: A loyal wife brings joy to her husband, and he will finish his years in peace.
text.NRSV: A loyal wife brings joy to her husband, and he will finish his years in peace.
text.DRB: Astrong woman delighteth her husband, and shall accomplish the years of his life in peace.

text.KJV: 26:3 A good wife is a good portion, which shall be giuen in the portion of them that feare the Lord.
text.NABRE: A good wife is a generous gift bestowed upon him who fears the Lord.
text.NRSV: A good wife is a great blessing; she will be granted among the blessings of the man who fears the Lord.
text.DRB: Agood woman is a good portion, in the good portion of them that fear God shall she be given to a man for good deeds:
And the heart of rich and poor is good, at all time their countenance is merry.
text.NABRE: Whether rich or poor, his heart is content, a smile ever on his face.

text.DRB: Of three things my heart hath been afraid, and at the fourth my face hath trembled:
text.NABRE: There are three things I dread, and a fourth which terrifies me: Public slander, the gathering of a mob, and false accusation—all harder to bear than death.

text.DRB: The betraying of a city, and a gathering together of the people:
text.NABRE: A wife jealous of another wife is heartache and mourning; everyone feels the lash of her tongue.

text.DRB: false calumny, all more grievous than death.
text.NABRE: A wicked wife is a chafing yoke; taking hold of her is like grasping a scorpion.

text.DRB: Ajealous woman is the sorrow and mourning of the heart.
text.NABRE: A drunken wife arouses great anger, for she does not hide her shame.

text.DRB: In a jealous woman is a scourge of the tongue, communicating with all.
text.NABRE: By her haughty stare and her eyelids an unchaste wife can be recognized.

text.DRB: As a yoke of oxen that is moved, so also a wicked woman: he that holdeth her, is as he that taketh hold of a scorpion.
text.NABRE: Keep a strict watch over an unruly wife, lest, finding an opportunity, she use it;

text.DRB: Awoman given to drunkenness is great anger: & her contumely and turpitude shall not be hid.
text.NABRE: Watch out for her impudent eye, and do not be surprised if she betrays you:

text.DRB: The fornication of a woman shall be known in the lifting up of her eyes, and in her eye-lids.
text.NABRE: As a thirsty traveler opens his mouth and drinks from any water nearby, So she sits down before every tent peg and opens her quiver for every arrow.

text.KJV: 26:13 The grace of a wife delighteth her husband, and her discretion will fat his bones.
text.NABRE: A gracious wife delights her husband; her thoughtfulness puts flesh on his bones.
text.NRSV: A wife’s charm delights her husband, and her skill puts fat on his bones.
text.DRB: On thy daughter that turneth not away herself, set sure watch: lest occasion found she abuse herself.

text.KJV: 26:14 A silent and louing woman is a gift of the Lord, and there is nothing so much worth, as a mind well instructed.
text.NABRE: A silent wife is a gift from the Lord; nothing is worth more than her self-discipline.
text.NRSV: A silent wife is a gift from the Lord, and nothing is so precious as her self-discipline.
text.DRB: Take heed of the impudency of her eyes, and marvel not if she contemn thee.

As a wayfaring man that thirsteth, will she open her mouth to the fountain, and will drink of every water that is next, & will sit against every hedge, and open her quiver against every arrow, until she fail.
text.NABRE: A modest wife is a supreme blessing; no scales can weigh the worth of her chastity.

text.DRB: The grace of a diligent woman shall delight her husband, and shall fat his bones.
text.NABRE: The sun rising in the Lord’s heavens— the beauty of a good wife in her well-ordered home.

text.DRB: Her discipline is the gift of God.
text.NABRE: The light which shines above the holy lampstand— a beautiful face on a stately figure.

text.DRB: Awise and still woman, there is no exchange for a soul instructed.
text.NABRE: Golden columns on silver bases— so her shapely legs and steady feet.

text.DRB: Aholy, and shamefast woman, is grace upon grace.
And all weight is not worthy a continent soul.
As the sun rising to the world in the highest places of God, so is the beauty of a good woman for an ornament of her house.
Alamp shining upon the holy candlestick, & the beauty of the face upon stayed age.
Pillars of gold upon feet of silver, and stable feet upon the soles of a stayed woman.
Eternal foundations upon a sound rock, and the commandments of God in the heart of a holy woman.
At two things my heart is grieved, and at the third anger is come upon me:
Aman of war decaying by poverty: and a wise man contemned:
and he that transgresseth from justice to sin, God hath prepared him to the sword.

text.KJV: 26:28 There be two things that grieue my heart: and the third maketh me angry: a man of warre that suffereth pouerty, and men of vnderstanding that are not set by: and one that returneth from righteousnesse to sinne: the Lord prepareth such a one for the sword.
text.NRSV: Two things grieve my heart, and a third makes me angry: a warrior in want through poverty, intelligent men who are treated with contempt, and a man who turns back from righteousness to sin—the Lord will prepare him for the sword!
text.DRB: Two sorts have appeared unto me hard and dangerous, a merchant is hardly rid of negligence: and a viteler shall not be justified from the sins of the lips.

text.KJV: 26:29 A merchant shall hardly keepe himselfe from doing wrong: and an huckster shall not be freed from sinne.
text.NRSV: A merchant can hardly keep from wrongdoing, nor is a tradesman innocent of sin.

text.KJV: 27:1 Many haue sinned for a smal matter: & he that seeketh for abundance will turne his eies away.
text.NABRE: Chapter 27 For the sake of profit many sin, and the struggle for wealth blinds the eyes.
text.NRSV: Many have sinned for the sake of profit, and one who seeks to grow rich must turn away his eyes.
text.DRB: Through poverty many have offended: and he that seeketh to be made rich, turneth away his eye.

text.KJV: 27:2 As a naile sticketh fast betweene the ioynings of the stones: so doth sinne sticke close betweene buying and selling.
text.NABRE: A stake will be driven between fitted stones— sin will be wedged in between buying and selling.
text.NRSV: As a stake is driven firmly into a fissure between stones, so sin is wedged in between selling and buying.
text.DRB: As a stake is fastened in the midst of stones compact together, so also in the midst of selling and buying, sin shall be straitened.

text.KJV: 27:3 Unlesse a man hold himselfe diligently in the feare of the Lord, his house shall soone be ouerthrowen.
text.NABRE: Unless one holds fast to the fear of the Lord, with sudden swiftness will one’s house be thrown down.
text.NRSV: Unless a person holds zealously to the fear of the Lord, his house will be quickly overthrown.
text.DRB: Sin shall be destroyed with the sinner.

text.KJV: 27:4 As when one sifteth with a sieue, the refuse remaineth, so the filth of man in his talke.
text.NABRE: When a sieve is shaken, the husks appear; so do people’s faults when they speak.
text.NRSV: When a sieve is shaken, the refuse appears; so do a person’s faults when he speaks.
text.DRB: If thou hold not thyself instantly in the fear of our Lord, thy house shall quickly be subverted.

text.KJV: 27:5 The furnace prooueth the potters vessell: so the triall of man is in his reasoning.
text.NABRE: The furnace tests the potter’s vessels; the test of a person is in conversation.
text.NRSV: The kiln tests the potter’s vessels; so the test of a person is in his conversation.
text.DRB: As in the shaking of a sieve the dust will remain: so the perplexity of a man in his cogitation.

text.KJV: 27:6 The fruite declareth if the tree haue beene dressed: so is the vtterance of a conceit in the heart of man.
text.NABRE: The fruit of a tree shows the care it has had; so speech discloses the bent of a person’s heart.
text.NRSV: Its fruit discloses the cultivation of a tree; so a person’s speech discloses the cultivation of his mind.
text.DRB: The furnace trieth the potter's vessels, and the tentation of tribulation just men.
As the husbandry about a tree sheweth the fruit thereof, so a word out of the thought of the heart of man.
text.NABRE: Praise no one before he speaks, for it is then that people are tested.

text.DRB: Praise not a man before full discourse, for this is the trial of men.
text.NABRE: If you strive after justice, you will attain it, and wear it like a splendid robe.

text.DRB: If thou follow justice, thou shalt apprehend it: and shalt put it on as a long robe of honour, and thou shalt dwell with it: and it shall protect thee for ever, and in the day of acknowledging thou shalt find steadfastness.
text.NABRE: Birds nest with their own kind, and honesty comes to those who work at it.

text.DRB: The fowls flock together to their like: and truth shall return to them that work it.
text.NABRE: A lion lies in wait for prey, so does sin for evildoers.

text.DRB: The lion always lieth in wait for a prey: so sins for them that work iniquities.
text.NABRE: The conversation of the godly is always wisdom, but the fool changes like the moon.

text.DRB: Aholy man continueth in wisdom as the sun: for a fool is changed as the moon.
text.NABRE: Limit the time you spend among the stupid, but frequent the company of the thoughtful.

text.DRB: In the midst of the unwise keep the word till his time: but in the midst of deep considerers be continually.
text.NABRE: The conversation of fools is offensive, and their laughter is wanton sin.

text.DRB: The narration of sinners is odious, & their laughter is in the delights of sin.
text.NABRE: Their oath-filled talk makes the hair stand on end, and their brawls make one stop the ears.

text.DRB: Speech that sweareth much shall make the hair of the head to stand upright: and his lack of reverence is the stopping of the ears.
text.NABRE: The wrangling of the proud ends in bloodshed, and their cursing is painful to hear.

text.KJV: 27:16 Who so discouereth secrets, looseth his credit: and shall neuer find friend to his minde.
text.NABRE: Whoever betrays a secret destroys confidence, and will never find a congenial friend.
text.NRSV: One who discloses secrets destroys confidence, and will never find a congenial friend.
text.DRB: Shedding of blood is in the brawling of the proud: and their cursing is a grievous hearing.

text.KJV: 27:17 Loue thy friend, and be faithfull vnto him: but if thou bewrayest his secrets, follow no more after him.
text.NABRE: Cherish your friend, keep faith with him; but if you betray his secrets, do not go after him;
text.NRSV: Love your friend and keep faith with him; but if you disclose his secrets, do not follow after him.
text.DRB: He that discloseth the secret of a friend loseth credit, and he shall not find a friend to his mind.
Love thy neighbour, and be joined with him in fidelity.
text.NABRE: For as one might kill another, you have killed your neighbor’s friendship.

text.DRB: But if thou discover his secrets, thou shalt not pursue after him.
text.NABRE: Like a bird released from your hand, you have let your friend go and cannot recapture him.

text.DRB: For as a man that loseth his friend, so also he that loseth the friendship of his neighbour.
text.NABRE: Do not go after him, for he is far away, and has escaped like a gazelle from a snare.

text.DRB: And as he that letteth a bird go out of his hand, so hast thou left thy neighbour, & shalt not take him.
text.NABRE: For a wound can be bandaged, and an insult forgiven, but whoever betrays secrets does hopeless damage.

text.KJV: 27:22 He that winketh with the eies worketh euil, and he that knoweth him will depart from him.
text.NABRE: Malice, Anger and Vengeance Whoever has shifty eyes plots mischief and those who know him will keep their distance;
text.NRSV: Whoever winks with the eye plots mischief, and those who know him will keep their distance.
text.DRB: Follow him not, because he is far absent, for he is fled, as a doe out of the snare: because his soul is wounded.
Thou canst no more bind him, and of a curse there is reconciliation:
text.NABRE: In your presence he uses honeyed talk, and admires your words, But later he changes his tone and twists the words to your ruin.

text.DRB: but to disclose the secrets of a friend, is the desperation of an unhappy soul.
text.NABRE: I have hated many things but not as much as him, and the Lord hates him as well.

text.DRB: He that winketh with the eye, forgeth wicked things, and no man will cast him off:
text.NABRE: A stone falls back on the head of the one who throws it high, and a treacherous blow causes many wounds.

text.DRB: in the sight of thine eyes he will sweeten his mouth, and will be in admiration upon thy words: but at the last he will pervert his mouth, and in thy words he will lay a scandal.
text.NABRE: Whoever digs a pit falls into it, and whoever lays a snare is caught in it.

text.DRB: I have heard many things, & have not esteemed them equal to him, and our Lord will hate him.
text.NABRE: The evil anyone does will recoil on him without knowing how it came upon him.

text.DRB: He that casteth a stone on high, it will fall upon his head: & the deceitful stroke will divide the wounds of the deceitful.
text.NABRE: Mockery and abuse will befall the arrogant, and vengeance lies in wait for them like a lion.

text.DRB: He that diggeth a pit, shall fall into it: and he that setteth a stone for his neighbour, shall stumble on it: & he that layeth a snare for another, shall perish in it.
text.NABRE: Those who rejoice in the downfall of the godly will be caught in a snare, and pain will consume them before they die.

text.DRB: To a man that doth most wicked counsel, it shall be turned upon himself, and he shall not know from whence it cometh to him.
text.NABRE: Wrath and anger, these also are abominations, yet a sinner holds on to them.

text.DRB: Derision & reproach of the proud, and vengeance as a lion shall lie in wait for him.
They shall perish in a snare that are delighted with the fall of the just: and sorrow shall consume them before they die.
Anger and fury, both are execrable, and the sinful man shall be subject to them.

text.KJV: 28:1 He that reuengeth shall find vengeance from the Lord, and he will surely keepe his sinnes (in remembrance.)
text.NABRE: Chapter 28 The vengeful will face the Lord’s vengeance; indeed he remembers their sins in detail.
text.NRSV: The vengeful will face the Lord’s vengeance, for he keeps a strict account of their sins.
text.DRB: He that will be revenged, shall find revenge of our Lord, and keeping he will keep his sin.

text.KJV: 28:2 Forgiue thy neighbour the hurt that he hath done vnto thee, so shall thy sinnes also be forgiuen when thou prayest.
text.NABRE: Forgive your neighbor the wrong done to you; then when you pray, your own sins will be forgiven.
text.NRSV: Forgive your neighbor the wrong he has done, and then your sins will be pardoned when you pray.
text.DRB: Forgive thy neighbour hurting thee: & then shall thy sins be loosed to thee when thou prayest.

text.KJV: 28:3 One man beareth hatred against another, and doeth he seeke pardon from the Lord?
text.NABRE: Does anyone nourish anger against another and expect healing from the Lord?
text.NRSV: Does anyone harbor anger against another, and expect healing from the Lord?
text.DRB: Man to man reserveth anger, and doth he seek remedy of God?

text.KJV: 28:4 Hee sheweth no mercy to a man, which is like himselfe: and doeth hee aske forgiuenesse of his owne sinnes?
text.NABRE: Can one refuse mercy to a sinner like oneself, yet seek pardon for one’s own sins?
text.NRSV: If one has no mercy toward another like himself, can he then beg for pardon of his own sins?
text.DRB: He hath not mercy on a man like unto himself, and doth he entreat for his own sins?
Himself whereas he is flesh, reserveth anger, and doth he ask propitiation of God? Who by prayer shall obtain for his sin?
text.NABRE: If a mere mortal cherishes wrath, who will forgive his sins?

text.KJV: 28:6 Remember thy end, and let enimitie cease, corruption and death, and abide in the Commandements.
text.NABRE: Remember your last days and set enmity aside; remember death and decay, and cease from sin!
text.NRSV: Remember the end of your life, and set enmity aside; remember corruption and death, and be true to the commandments.
text.DRB: Remember the last things, & cease to be at enmity:
for consumption and death are imminent in his commandments.
text.NABRE: Remember the commandments and do not be angry with your neighbor; remember the covenant of the Most High, and overlook faults.

text.DRB: Remember the fear of God, and be not angry with thy neighbour.
text.NABRE: Avoid strife and your sins will be fewer, for the hot-tempered kindle strife;

text.DRB: Remember the testament of the High, & contemn the ignorance of thy neighbour.
text.NABRE: The sinner disrupts friendships and sows discord among those who are at peace.

text.DRB: Refrain thyself from strife, and thou shalt diminish thy sins:
text.NABRE: The more the wood, the greater the fire, the more the cruelty, the fiercer the strife; The greater the strength, the sterner the anger, the greater the wealth, the greater the wrath.

text.DRB: for an angry man kindleth strife, and a sinful man will trouble his friends, and in the midst of them that are at peace he will cast in enmity.
text.NABRE: Pitch and resin make fire flare up, and a hasty quarrel provokes bloodshed.

text.KJV: 28:12 If thou blow the sparke, it shall burne: if thou spit vpon it, it shall bee quenched, and both these come out of thy mouth.
text.NABRE: The Evil Tongue If you blow on a spark, it turns into flame, if you spit on it, it dies out; yet both you do with your mouth!
text.NRSV: If you blow on a spark, it will glow; if you spit on it, it will be put out; yet both come out of your mouth.
text.DRB: For according to the wood of the forest, so the fire burneth: and according to the power of a man, so shall his anger be, and according to his substance he will increase his anger.

text.KJV: 28:13 Curse the whisperer, and double tongued: for such haue destroyed many that were at peace.
text.NABRE: Cursed be gossips and the double-tongued, for they destroy the peace of many.
text.NRSV: Curse the whisperer and the double-tongued, for they have destroyed many who were at peace.
text.DRB: Hasty contention kindleth a fire: & hasty strife sheddeth blood: and an ill testifying tongue bringeth death.
If thou blow upon a spark, it will burn as a fire: and if thou spit thereon, it shall be quenched: both proceed out of the mouth.
text.NABRE: A meddlesome tongue subverts many, and makes them refugees among peoples. It destroys strong cities, and overthrows the houses of the great.

text.DRB: The whisperer & double tongued is accurst: for he hath troubled many that were at peace.
text.NABRE: A meddlesome tongue drives virtuous women from their homes, and robs them of the fruit of their toil.

text.DRB: A third tongue hath moved many, and dispersed them from nation into nation.
text.NABRE: Whoever heed it will find no rest, nor will they dwell in peace.

text.DRB: It hath destroyed the walled cities of the rich, and hath digged down the houses of great men.
text.NABRE: A blow from a whip raises a welt, but a blow from the tongue will break bones.

text.KJV: 28:18 Many haue fallen by the edge of the sword: but not so many as haue fallen by the tongue.
text.NABRE: Many have fallen by the edge of the sword, but not as many as by the tongue.
text.NRSV: Many have fallen by the edge of the sword, but not as many as have fallen because of the tongue.
text.DRB: It hath cut the forces of peoples, and undone strong nations.
Athird tongue hath cast out many women, and deprived them of their labours.
text.NABRE: Happy the one who is sheltered from it, and has not endured its wrath; Who has not borne its yoke nor been bound with its chains.

text.DRB: He that regardeth it, shall not have rest, neither shall he have a friend in whom he may repose.
text.NABRE: For its yoke is a yoke of iron, and its chains are chains of bronze;

text.DRB: The stroke of a whip maketh a blue mark: but the stroke of the tongue will break the bones.
text.NABRE: The death it inflicts is an evil death, even Sheol is preferable to it.

text.DRB: Many have fallen by the edge of the sword, but not so as they that have perished by their tongue.
text.NABRE: It will have no power over the godly, nor will they be burned in its flame.

text.DRB: Blessed is he that is covered from a wicked tongue, that hath not passed into the anger thereof, and that hath not drawn the yoke thereof, and hath not been tied in the bands thereof:
text.NABRE: But those who forsake the Lord will fall victim to it, as it burns among them unquenchably; It will hurl itself against them like a lion, and like a leopard, it will tear them to pieces.

text.DRB: for the yoke of it is a yoke of iron: and the band of it is a band of brass.
text.NABRE: As you fence in your property with thorns, so make a door and a bolt for your mouth.

text.DRB: The death of it, is a most wicked death: and hell is more profitable than it.
text.NABRE: As you lock up your silver and gold, so make balances and scales for your words.

text.DRB: The continuance of it shall not be permanent, but it shall obtain the ways of the unjust: and it shall not burn the just in the flame thereof.
text.NABRE: Take care not to slip by your tongue and fall victim to one lying in ambush.

text.DRB: They that forsake God, shall fall into it, and it shall burn in them, and shall not be quenched, and it shall be sent in upon them as a lion, and as a leopard it shall hurt them.
Hedge thine ears with thorns, and hear not a wicked tongue, and make doors to thy mouth, and locks.
Lay together thy silver, and make balance to thy words, and right bridles to thy mouth:
and take heed lest perhaps thou slip in thy tongue, & fall in the sight of the enemies, that lie in wait for thee, and thy fall be uncurable unto death.

text.KJV: 29:1 Hee that is mercifull, will lende vnto his neighbour, and hee that strengthneth his hande, keepeth the Commandements.
text.NABRE: Chapter 29 Loans, Alms and Surety The merciful lend to their neighbor, by holding out a helping hand, they keep the commandments.
text.NRSV: The merciful lend to their neighbors; by holding out a helping hand they keep the commandments.
text.DRB: He that lendeth to his neighbour, doth mercy: and he that prevaileth with hand, keepeth the commandments.

text.KJV: 29:2 Lend to thy neighbour in time of his need, and pay thou thy neighbour againe in due season.
text.NABRE: Lend to your neighbor in his time of need, and pay back your neighbor in time.
text.NRSV: Lend to your neighbor in his time of need; pay your neighbor back at the appointed time.
text.DRB: Lend to thy neighbour in the time of his necessity, and again repay thy neighbour in his time.
Confirm thy word, and do faithfully with him: & thou shalt find at all time, that which is necessary for thee.
text.NABRE: Keep your promise and be honest with him, and at all times you will find what you need.

text.DRB: Many have esteemed a thing lent as a thing found, and have given molestation to them that did help them.
text.NABRE: Many borrowers ask for a loan and cause trouble for those who help them.

text.DRB: Till they receive, they kiss the hands of the lender, and in promises they humble their voice:
text.NABRE: Till he gets a loan, he kisses the lender’s hand and speaks softly of his creditor’s money, But at time of payment, delays, makes excuses, and finds fault with the timing.

text.DRB: and in the time of repaying they will ask a time, and will speak words of tediousness and murmurings, and will make the time an excuse:
text.NABRE: If he can pay, the lender will recover barely half, and will consider that a windfall. If he cannot pay, the lender is cheated of his money and acquires an enemy at no extra charge; With curses and insults the borrower will repay, and instead of honor will repay with abuse.

text.DRB: and if he be able to pay, he will resist, he will pay scarce half of the whole, and will account it as a thing found:
text.NABRE: Many refuse to lend, not out of meanness, but from fear of being cheated needlessly.

text.KJV: 29:8 Yet haue thou patience with a man in poore estate, and delay not to shew him mercy.
text.NABRE: But with those in humble circumstances be patient; do not keep them waiting for your alms.
text.NRSV: Be patient with someone in humble circumstances, and do not keep him waiting for your alms.
text.DRB: but if not, he will defraud him of his money, and possess him an enemy without cause:
and will repay him reproaches and curses, and for honour and benefit will repay him contumely.
text.NABRE: Because of the commandment, help the poor, and in their need, do not send them away empty-handed.

text.KJV: 29:10 Lose thy money for thy brother and thy friend, and let it not rust vnder a stone to be lost.
text.NABRE: Lose your money for relative or friend; do not hide it under a stone to rot.
text.NRSV: Lose your silver for the sake of a brother or a friend, and do not let it rust under a stone and be lost.
text.DRB: Many have not lent, not because of wickedness, but they were afraid to be defrauded without cause.

text.KJV: 29:11 Lay vp thy treasure according to the commandements of the most high, and it shall bring thee more profite then golde.
text.NABRE: Dispose of your treasure according to the commandments of the Most High, and that will profit you more than the gold.
text.NRSV: Lay up your treasure according to the commandments of the Most High, and it will profit you more than gold.
text.DRB: But yet upon the humble be stronger of mind, & for alms defer him not.
Because of the commandment receive the poor: and because of his poverty, send him not away empty.
text.NABRE: Store up almsgiving in your treasury, and it will save you from every evil.

text.DRB: Lose money for thy brother and thy friend: and hide it not under a stone unto perdition.
text.NABRE: Better than a mighty shield and a sturdy spear it will fight for you against the enemy.

text.DRB: Put thy treasure in the precepts of the Highest, & it shall profit thee more than gold.
text.NABRE: A good person will be surety for a neighbor, but whoever has lost a sense of shame will fail him.

text.DRB: Shut up alms in the heart of the poor, and the same shall obtain for thee against all evil.
text.NABRE: Do not forget the kindness of your backer, for he has given his very life for you.

text.DRB: The alms a man giveth is as a purse with him: it will conserve his grace as the ball of the eye.
text.NABRE: A sinner will turn the favor of a pledge into misfortune,

text.DRB: And furthermore will raise up and render reward to every one upon their heads.
text.NABRE: and the ungrateful will abandon his rescuer.

text.DRB: Above the shield of the mighty, & above the spear, it shall fight against thine enemy.
text.NABRE: Going surety has ruined many who were prosperous and tossed them about like waves of the sea; It has exiled the prominent and sent them wandering through foreign lands.

text.DRB: Agood man becometh surety for his neighbour: and he that hath lost shame, will leave him to himself.
text.NABRE: The sinner will come to grief through surety, and whoever undertakes too much will fall into lawsuits.

text.DRB: Forget not the kindness of a surety: for he hath given his life for thee.
text.NABRE: Help your neighbor according to your means, but take care lest you fall yourself.

text.KJV: 29:21 The chiefe thing for life is water and bread, and clothing, and an house to couer shame.
text.NABRE: Frugality and Its Rewards Life’s prime needs are water, bread, and clothing, and also a house for decent privacy.
text.NRSV: The essentials of life are water and bread and clothing, and a house to provide privacy.
text.DRB: The sinner and unclean person flyeth from his surety.

text.KJV: 29:22 Better is the life of a poore man in a meane cottage, then delicate fare in another mans house.
text.NABRE: Better is the life of the poor under the shadow of their own roof than sumptuous banquets among strangers.
text.NRSV: Better is the life of a poor person under the shelter of his own roof than sumptuous food in another’s house.
text.DRB: Asinner counteth the goods of his surety to himself: and unthankful in mind, will forsake him that delivered him.
Aman is surety for his neighbour: and when he hath lost shame, he shall be forsaken of him.
text.NABRE: Whether little or much, be content with what you have: then you will hear no reproach as a parasite.

text.DRB: Naughty suretyship hath undone many that were in good case, and hath tossed them as a wave of the sea.
text.NABRE: It is a miserable life to go from house to house, for where you are a guest you dare not open your mouth.

text.DRB: Whurling round about, it hath made mighty men to remove, and they have wandered in strange nations.
text.NABRE: You will entertain and provide drink without being thanked; besides, you will hear these bitter words:

text.DRB: Asinner that transgresseth the commandment of our Lord, shall fall into naughty suretyship: and he that endeavoureth to do many things, shall fall into judgement.
text.NABRE: “Come here, you parasite, set the table, let me eat the food you have there!

text.DRB: Recover thy neighbour according to thy power, and take heed to thyself that thou fall not.
text.NABRE: Go away, you parasite, for one more worthy; for my relative’s visit I need the room!”

text.KJV: 29:28 These things are grieuous to a man of vnderstanding: the vpbraiding of house-roome, and reproching of the lender.
text.NABRE: Painful things to a sensitive person are rebuke as a parasite and insults from creditors.
text.NRSV: To a sensible person these things are intolerable: expression of grievance from a household, and the insults of a moneylender.
text.DRB: The beginning of man's life water & bread, and garment, and house covering his turpitude.
Better is the poor man's fare under a roof of boards, than sumptuous cheer in a strange place without a house.
Let the least thing please thee in stead of a great, and thou shalt not hear the reproach of peregrination.
It is a naughty life to change lodging from house to house: and where he shall lodge, he shall not deal boldly, nor open his mouth.
He shall lodge, and feed, and make the unthankful drink, and beside these things he shall hear bitter words.
Pass thou stranger & furnish the table, & with the things thou hast in thy hand, feed the rest.
Depart from the presence of the honour of my friends: for the necessity of my house my brother is to be lodged with me.
These things be grievous to a man that hath understanding: rebuke for the house, and the reproach of the lender.

text.KJV: 30:1 Hee that loueth his sonne, causeth him oft to feele the rodde, that hee may haue ioy of him in the end.
text.NABRE: Chapter 30 The Training of Children Whoever loves a son will chastise him often, that he may be his joy when he grows up.
text.NRSV: He who loves his son will whip him often, so that he may rejoice at the way he turns out.
text.DRB: He that loveth his son, doth accustom him to stripes, that he may rejoice in his later end, and not grope after the doors of his neighbours.

text.KJV: 30:2 He that chastiseth his sonne, shall haue ioy in him, and shall reioyce of him among his acquaintance.
text.NABRE: Whoever disciplines a son will benefit from him, and boast of him among acquaintances.
text.NRSV: He who disciplines his son will profit by him, and will boast of him among acquaintances.
text.DRB: He that teacheth his son, shall be praised in him, & in the midst of them of his household he shall glory in him.
He that teacheth his son, doth cast the enemy into emulation, and in the midst of his friends he shall glory in him.
text.NABRE: Whoever educates a son will make his enemy jealous, and rejoice in him among his friends.

text.DRB: His father is dead, & he is as it were not dead: for he hath left behind him the like to himself.
text.NABRE: At the father’s death, he will seem not dead, for he leaves after him one like himself,

text.DRB: In his life he saw and rejoiced in him: in his death he was not made sorry, neither was he confounded before the enemies.
text.NABRE: Whom he looked upon through life with joy, and in death, without regret.

text.DRB: For he left a defender of his house against the enemies, & one that should render thank to his friends.
text.NABRE: Against his enemies he has left an avenger, and one to repay his friends with kindness.

text.DRB: For the souls of his sons he will bind up his wounds, & at every voice his bowels shall be troubled.
text.NABRE: Whoever spoils a son will have wounds to bandage, and will suffer heartache at every cry.

text.KJV: 30:8 An horse not broken becommeth headstrong: and a childe left to himselfe will be wilfull.
text.NABRE: An untamed horse turns out stubborn; and a son left to himself grows up unruly.
text.NRSV: A horse untamed grows stubborn, and a son left to himself grows headstrong.
text.DRB: An untamed horse becometh stubborn, and a dissolute child will become heady.
Pamper thy son, and he will make thee afraid: play with him, and he will make thee sorrowful.
text.NABRE: Pamper a child and he will be a terror for you, indulge him, and he will bring you grief.

text.DRB: Laugh not with him, lest thou be sorry, and at the last thy teeth shall be on edge.
text.NABRE: Do not laugh with him lest you share sorrow with him, and in the end you will gnash your teeth.

text.DRB: Give him not power in his youth, and contemn not his cogitations.
text.NABRE: Do not give him his own way in his youth, and do not ignore his follies.

text.DRB: Curb his neck in youth, and knock his sides whilst he is a child, lest perhaps he be hardened, and believe thee not, and he shall be sorrow of mind to thee.
text.NABRE: Bow down his head in his youth, beat his sides while he is still young, Lest he become stubborn and disobey you, and leave you disconsolate.

text.KJV: 30:13 Chastise thy sonne, and hold him to labour, lest his lewd behauiour be an offence vnto thee.
text.NABRE: Discipline your son and make heavy his yoke, lest you be offended by his shamelessness.
text.NRSV: Discipline your son and make his yoke heavy, so that you may not be offended by his shamelessness.
text.DRB: Teach thy son, and work in him, that thou offend not in his dishonesty.

text.KJV: 30:14 Better is the poore being sound and strong of constitution, then a rich man that is afflicted in his body.
text.NABRE: Health and Cheerfulness Better the poor in vigorous health than the rich with bodily ills.
text.NRSV: Better off is a poor man who is well and strong in constitution than a rich man who is severely afflicted in body.
text.DRB: Better is a poor man whole and strong of force, than a rich man weak and scourged with misery.

text.KJV: 30:15 Health and good state of body are aboue all gold, and a strong body aboue infinite wealth.
text.NABRE: I would rather have bodily health than any gold, and contentment of spirit than pearls.
text.NRSV: Health and vigor are better than any gold, and a robust body than countless riches.
text.DRB: The health of the soul in holiness of justice, is better than all gold and silver: and a sound body, than infinite revenues.
There is no riches above the riches of the health of the body: and there is no delight above the joy of the heart.
text.NABRE: No riches are greater than a healthy body; and no happiness than a joyful heart.

text.KJV: 30:17 Death is better then a bitter life, or continuall sickenesse.
text.NABRE: Better is death than a wretched life, everlasting sleep than constant illness.
text.NRSV: Death is better than a miserable life, and eternal rest than chronic sickness.
text.DRB: Better is death than a bitter life: and everlasting rest, than continual sickness.
Good things hid in a mouth that is shut, are as messes of meats set about a grave.
text.NABRE: Good things set before one who cannot eat are like food offerings placed before a tomb.

text.DRB: What shall sacrifice profit an idol? For neither shall he eat, nor smell:
text.NABRE: What good is an offering to an idol that can neither eat nor smell? So it is with the one being punished by the Lord,

text.DRB: so he that is chased away of our Lord, beareth the rewards of iniquity:
text.NABRE: who groans at what his eyes behold.

text.KJV: 30:21 Giue not ouer thy mind to heauinesse, and afflict not thy selfe in thine owne counsell.
text.NABRE: Do not give in to sadness, or torment yourself deliberately.
text.NRSV: Do not give yourself over to sorrow, and do not distress yourself deliberately.
text.DRB: seeing with his eyes, & groaning, as an eunuch embracing a virgin and sighing.
Give not heaviness to thy soul, & afflict not thyself in thy counsel.
text.NABRE: Gladness of heart is the very life of a person, and cheerfulness prolongs his days.

text.KJV: 30:23 Loue thine owne soule, and comfort thy heart, remoue sorrow far from thee: for sorrow hath killed many, and there is no profit therein.
text.NABRE: Distract yourself and renew your courage, drive resentment far away from you; For grief has killed many, and nothing is to be gained from resentment.
text.NRSV: Beguile yourself and comfort your heart, and drive sorrow far from you, for sorrow has destroyed many, and there is no profit in it.
text.DRB: Joyfulness of the heart, this is the life of a man, and a treasure without defect of holiness: and the joy of a man is long life.

text.KJV: 30:24 Enuie and wrath shorten the life, and carefulnesse bringeth age before the time.
text.NABRE: Envy and anger shorten one’s days, and anxiety brings on premature old age.
text.NRSV: Jealousy and anger shorten life, and anxiety brings on premature old age.
text.DRB: Have mercy on thine own soul, pleasing God, and refrain: and comfort thy heart in his holiness: and expel sorrow far from thee.
For sorrow hath killed many, and there is no profit in it.
text.NABRE: Those who are cheerful and merry at table benefit from their food.

text.DRB: Envy and anger diminish the days, and thought will bring old age before the time.
Amagnifical heart is good in banquets: for his banquets are made diligently.

text.KJV: 31:1 Watching for riches, consumeth the flesh, and the care therof driueth away sleepe.
text.NABRE: Chapter 31 The Proper Attitude Toward Riches Wakefulness over wealth wastes away the flesh, and anxiety over it drives away sleep.
text.NRSV: Wakefulness over wealth wastes the flesh, and anxiety about it drives away sleep.
text.DRB: Watching after honesty shall pine the flesh, & the thought thereof taketh away sleep.
The thought of foreknowledge turneth away the understanding, & grievous infirmity maketh a sober soul.
text.NABRE: Wakeful anxiety banishes slumber; more than a serious illness it disturbs repose.

text.DRB: The rich man hath laboured in gathering of substance together, & in his rest he shall be replenished with his goods.
text.NABRE: The rich labor to pile up wealth, and if they rest, it is to enjoy pleasure;

text.DRB: The poor man hath laboured in the diminishing of his living, and in the end he is made poor.
text.NABRE: The poor labor for a meager living, and if they ever rest, they become needy.

text.DRB: He that loveth gold shall not be justified: & he that followeth after corruption, shall be replenished of it.
text.NABRE: The lover of gold will not be free from sin; whoever pursues money will be led astray by it.

text.DRB: Many have been given into falls for gold, and their perdition hath come by the beauty thereof.
text.NABRE: Many have come to ruin for the sake of gold, yet destruction lay before their very eyes;

text.DRB: The gold of them that sacrifice is a wood of offence: woe to them, that follow after it, and every unwise man shall perish in it.
text.NABRE: It is a stumbling block for fools; any simpleton will be ensnared by it.

text.DRB: Blessed is the rich man that is found without spot: and that hath not gone after gold, nor hoped in money and treasures.
text.NABRE: Happy the rich person found without fault, who does not turn aside after wealth.

text.DRB: Who is this? & we will praise him, for he hath done marvelous things in his life.
text.NABRE: Who is he, that we may praise him? For he has done wonders among his people.
